en: As a result, we've seen lower quality and customers are super pissed, employees are pissed, and now it's starting to shrink market share."

speaker: Unknown
en: No.

speaker: Unknown
en: No wonder it collapsed."

speaker: Unknown
en: No.

speaker: Unknown
en: "Oh yeah, good idea."
zh: "噢，是的，好主意。"

speaker: Unknown
en: So this book is about what are the organizational equivalents of stainless steel.
zh: 所以这本书讲的是组织层面相当于不锈钢的东西是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the book is structured, not like a traditional business book, as you say, you picked up on that.

speaker: Unknown

en: You're actually going to give us answers and solutions because it sounds like, okay, this is impossible.

en: But you actually have-

en: ... you have some answers for what founders can do.

en: There's a whole viral thing on it.

speaker: Unknown

en: Okay.

en: All right.

en: I'm definitely worried.

en: Definitely worried to hear that.

en: I want to talk about two things that are probably in people's minds as they listen to this conversation and even think about buying your book.

en: One is, okay, I am not going to let this happen to my company.
zh: 一个是，好吧，我不会让这种情况发生在我的公司。

en: This is-

en: Oh, sure.

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.

speaker: Unknown
en: ... oh, other people, they're weak, maybe they're of loose values.

zh: 另一个想法就像是，我真的需要做这个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so many companies that are killing it.

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think they've done any of this.

speaker: Unknown
en: Why do I even need to pay attention to this?

speaker: Unknown
en: So maybe let's start with that first one or this idea of, and this is a core thesis of your book.
zh: 所以也许让我们从第一个开始，或者这个想法，这是你书中的核心论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not like an ethical values thing.

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a structural element of building businesses in the US.

speaker: Unknown

en: If you don't get this right, no other decision you make about your company will matter for the long term because you're not going to be the one making it.

en: Just statistically speaking, everyone's being told by their lawyers, their bankers, their VCs, everybody that you're the exception.

en: It's not going to happen to you, but statistically speaking, you're much more likely to be in the 80% than the 20%.

en: They were planning IPO and I built the long-term stock exchange.

en: I've done a bunch of stuff that people come to me for advice about these things.

speaker: Unknown
en: They wanted advice.

speaker: Unknown
en: How can we structure the IPO?

speaker: Unknown
en: We want to think long-term, we're really a mission-driven company, et cetera, et cetera.

en: And I was going through their governance documents.

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Oh, good.

speaker: Unknown
en: And the founder was really concerned.

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Okay, we're going to definitely do something about this.
zh: 他说，“好吧，我们肯定要对此做点什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to fix it up."
zh: 我们会把它修好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But then he called me back a few months later.

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like...
zh: 他说……

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Well, what are you going to do?"
zh: 我说，“那么，你打算怎么做？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "You know what?
zh: 他说，“你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I talked to my bankers, talked to my lawyers, talked to my CFO, talked to my GC, talked to my VCs, talked my growth VCs.

speaker: Unknown
en: You know what they all said?

speaker: Unknown
en: They all said, 'Man, Eric is such a downer.
zh: 他们都说，‘伙计，Eric 真是个扫兴的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: If he really believed in your vision, if he really saw how special you were, he wouldn't talk like that.
zh: 如果他真的相信你的愿景，如果他真的看到你有多特别，他就不会那样说话。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're the exception.'"
zh: 你是例外。’”

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Okay, man, good luck."
zh: 我说，“好吧，伙计，祝你好运。”

speaker: Unknown
en: This company went public, had a very successful IPO.
zh: 这家公司上市了， IPO 非常成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people made a lot of money.
zh: 很多人赚了很多钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then five months into their IPO, a competitor gets acquired and the whole category, everyone freaks out.
zh: 然后在上市五个月后，一个竞争对手被收购，整个类别，大家都恐慌了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stock price collapses and the founder is ousted after five months as a public company.

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, if you read stories about the company, people were like, "Oh, he made all these mistakes.

speaker: Unknown
en: Their business model sucked.
zh: 他们的商业模式很烂。

speaker: Unknown
en: The company..." Blah, blah, blah.
zh: 这家公司……”等等，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did he make mistakes?
zh: 他犯错了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure he did.
zh: 我确信他做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Were there problems?
zh: 有问题吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure.
zh: 我确信。

speaker: Unknown
en: But had he really earned so little grace that he only got five months, the same people saying that the business model is horribly flawed, they invested in the company five months ago.
zh: 但他真的积攒了这么少的恩情，以至于只得到了五个月，同样是那些说商业模式有严重缺陷的人，他们在五个月前投资了这家公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Had it really changed so much in five months?
zh: 它在五个月内真的发生了这么大的变化吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what's going on.
zh: 这就是正在发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: These collapses are all around us and we're being told it's normal.
zh: 这些崩溃就在我们周围，我们却被告知这是正常的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is just the way it has to be, but it's not.

speaker: Unknown
en: These are choices about the specific structures, cultural practices, the management practices we do on the inside and the structural, the governance practices we do on the outside.
zh: 这些是关于具体结构、文化实践，我们在内部所做的管理实践，以及我们在外部所做的结构性、治理实践的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: On both of them, were being told to do things that are incredibly weak.

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, if you think you're going to be the exception, think again.

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to talk about what to do, these governance elements, but let's talk about this other critique that I imagine people have in their head of just like, is this like...

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, I need to figure out product market fit first.
zh: 好的，我需要先弄清楚产品市场契合度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: Worry about it later.

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly.

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much more I need to do.
zh: 我还有太多事情要做。

speaker: Unknown
en: The chances of this working are so low.
zh: 这件事成功的几率太低了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why am ...

speaker: Unknown
en: I have no time for anything else.
zh: 我没有时间做任何其他事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, totally.
zh: 哦，完全是。

speaker: Unknown
en: You said something interesting in the lead in which you said like, "Well, a lot of other companies seem to be killing it and they don't have these protections, so they're fine."
zh: 你在开场白里说了一些有趣的话，你说，“好吧，很多其他公司似乎做得很好，而且它们没有这些保护，所以它们很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I would actually check your math.

en: So many times when people give me this argument, they were like, "Well, such and such company..." Someone was like, "Cloudflare, they are just a normal company.

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't do this stuff."
zh: 他们不做这些事情。”

en: I'm like, "Check your math, buddy.

en: Cloudflare does many of the things that we're talking about.

speaker: Unknown
en: They're one of the examples in the book, so I picked that on purpose."
zh: 它们是书中的例子之一，所以我特意挑选了它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of companies that you don't necessarily instantly think of as do-gooder, mission-driven companies are actually very mission-driven in terms of how they're structured, and they are almost always protected by at least one of the governance structures from this book.
zh: 很多你不一定立刻认为是行善者、使命驱动的公司，实际上在结构方面是非常使命驱动的，而且它们几乎总是受到本书中至少一种治理结构的保护。

speaker: Unknown
en: So check your math.
zh: 所以检查一下你的判断。

speaker: Unknown
en: People used to tell me Costco was an example that wasn't protected, so much so that I was surprised to learn that it was embodied in a governance fortress.
zh: 人们过去常告诉我 Costco 是一个不受保护的例子，以至于我很惊讶地得知它体现为一个治理堡垒。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll be surprised.

speaker: Unknown
en: If you do the math, if you look into it, you'll be surprised.
zh: 如果你做计算，如果你深入调查，你会感到惊讶。

speaker: Unknown
en: But let's talk about the product market fit thing because this one is really interesting to me.
zh: 但让我们谈谈产品市场契合度这件事，因为这对我来说真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Generally speaking, this is one of the most important ideas in the whole book.
zh: 一般来说，这是整本书里最重要的思想之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: The most important question about how to protect a product is not what protections it needs, but when those protections need to be enacted.
zh: 关于如何保护产品的最重要问题不是它需要什么保护，而是这些保护需要什么时候实施。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's basically like that old proverb about the best time to plant a tree was 40 years ago, but the next best time is now.
zh: 这基本上就像那句关于种树的最佳时间是40年前的古老谚语，但其次是现在。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is always too early until it's too late.
zh: 在太晚之前，总是太早。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll give you the example.
zh: 我给你举个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've seen this hundreds of times myself personally.
zh: 我个人亲眼见过这种情况数百次。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I've literally been in the room where this kind of stuff gets discussed and it starts like this.
zh: 所以我真的就在讨论这种事情的房间里，它是这样开始的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're incorporating your company, you talk to your lawyer, "Hey, I want to have..." These are called mission protective provisions in the law.
zh: 你正在注册你的公司，你和你的律师谈话，“嘿，我想有……”这些在法律中被称为使命保护条款。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I heard this guy on Lenny's podcast," and your lawyer's like, "Oh, not again.
zh: “我在 Lenny 的播客上听到了这家伙，”你的律师就像，“哦，又来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another guy, right?
zh: 又一个家伙，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many good ideas from Lenny's podcast are you going to tell me about?
zh: 你要告诉我多少个来自 Lenny 播客的好主意？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, fine.
zh: 好吧，好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's this one?"
zh: 这一次是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "He told me I need to have mission protective provisions."
zh: “他告诉我我需要有使命保护条款。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He'll pat you on the head and be like, "Oh, that's sweet, honey.
zh: 他会拍拍你的头，就像，“哦，那很甜蜜，亲爱的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's great.
zh: 太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, get product market fit, get some success.
zh: 是的，找到产品市场契合点，获得一些成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: Success is ultimately your source of leverage.
zh: 成功归根结底是你的杠杆来源。

speaker: Unknown
en: Success will protect you.
zh: 成功会保护你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't worry about it."
zh: 别担心这个。"

speaker: Unknown
en: You say, "Okay, great.
zh: 你说，"好的，太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But just thanks for letting me know."
zh: 但还是要谢谢你让我知道。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you raise some money.
zh: 现在你筹集了一些资金。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got these VCs on your board, say the same thing.
zh: 你的董事会里有了这些风投人，他们说着同样的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Yeah, I totally am with you.
zh: 他们就像，"是的，我完全支持你。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're on the same page.
zh: 我们的想法一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want the same thing you want.
zh: 我们想要的东西和你想要的一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: We invested because we believe in you as the founder.
zh: 我们投资是因为我们相信你这个创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no need to do this now.
zh: 现在没必要做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's just do it later.
zh: 我们以后再做吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do it when it's the more appropriate time."
zh: 在更合适的时间再做吧。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got a growth round.
zh: 你进行了一轮成长轮融资。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you got these bold contrarian growth VCs on your board.
zh: 现在你的董事会里有了这些大胆的反常规成长型风投人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "I don't know.
zh: 他们就像，"我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: You might not want to be too different from everybody else.
zh: 你可能不想和其他人太不一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Might make it hard to raise money."
zh: 这可能会让融资变得困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, "I thought you were a bold contrarian.
zh: 你就像，"我以为你是一个大胆的反常规者。

speaker: Unknown
en: What?"
zh: 什么？"

speaker: Unknown
en: "Okay, don't worry about it now.
zh: "好的，现在别担心。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can always do it later."
zh: 我们总可以以后再做。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you're doing IPO prep.
zh: 现在你在做 IPO 准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you got bankers and lawyers and you've got a GC and you got... And they're all like, "Yep, yep.
zh: 现在你有了银行家、律师，你有了总法律顾问，你还有……而且他们都像，"是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a great thing to bundle with the IPO itself.
zh: 这是一个可以和 IPO 本身捆绑在一起的好东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't need to worry about it now.
zh: 你现在不需要担心这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: First, we got to land the plane, let's get our house in order."
zh: 首先，我们要让飞机落地，让我们把家里的事情整理好。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Blah, blah, blah.
zh: 等等，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway, I've actually been in the room where the founder sits with the CFO and now it's like IPO planning, roadshow.
zh: 无论如何，我实际上真的就在那个房间里，创始人和 CFO 坐在一起，现在是 IPO 规划，路演。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here we go.
zh: 我们开始了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's go time.
zh: 到了行动的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're about to file the papers.
zh: 我们即将提交文件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the founder's like, "Hey, whatever happened to those mission protective provisions?"
zh: 创始人就像，"嘿，那些使命保护条款后来怎么样了？"

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, "I wanted to really make sure we had our customers could participate in our IPO and I want a broadly shared prosperity and I want this thing for our employees.
zh: 你就像，"我当时真的想确保我们的客户能参与我们的 IPO，我想要广泛共享繁荣，我想要这个为我们的员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have all this good stuff I want to do.
zh: 我有所有这些我想做的好东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did we do any of that stuff?
zh: 我们做了那些事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't see it in the S1."
zh: 我在 S1 里没看到。"

speaker: Unknown
en: The CFO's like, "Oh, you were serious about that?
zh: CFO 就像，"哦，你是认真的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, sorry, man.
zh: 哦，抱歉，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should have said something.
zh: 你应该早点说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it's too late."
zh: 现在太晚了。"

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, "Wait a minute, but when I talked to you about it last year, you said it was too early."
zh: 你就像，"等一下，但我去年和你谈这件事时，你说还太早。"

speaker: Unknown
en: "Yeah, it was.
zh: "是的，确实太早。

speaker: Unknown
en: But now it's too late."
zh: 但现在太晚了。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Was it ever the right time?
zh: 曾经有过合适的时机吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, it is never the right time to do this.
zh: 不，做这件事从来就没有合适的时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you put this off, you will eventually find yourself in a situation where you can no longer do it.
zh: 如果你把这件事推迟，你最终会发现自己陷入无法再做的境地。

speaker: Unknown
en: You will have lost the leverage.
zh: 你将失去筹码。

speaker: Unknown
en: Success will not protect you because success is what makes you a target.
zh: 成功不会保护你，因为成功正是让你成为靶子的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: That story I told you about the five months CEO got fired.
zh: 我告诉过你的那个关于 CEO 被解雇的五个月的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That company, everyone who worked on that IPO, every banker, every lawyer, the CFO, everybody, they profited from all the transaction volume that that company generated on the way up and on the way down.
zh: 那家公司，每个参与那次 IPO 的人，每个银行家，每个律师，CFO，每个人，他们都从那家公司一路上升和一路下跌的过程中产生的所有交易量中获利了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all fine.
zh: 他们都安然无恙。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all onto the next IPO.
zh: 他们都继续进行下一个 IPO 了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like carnivores.
zh: 他们就像食肉动物。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're onto the next thing to feed on.
zh: 他们继续寻找下一个可以捕食的对象。

speaker: Unknown
en: Meanwhile, the customers, the employees, the people that cared about their company were not so lucky.
zh: 与此同时，客户、员工、那些关心他们公司的人就没那么幸运了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Damn.
zh: 该死。

speaker: Unknown
en: That hit me.
zh: 这触动了我。

speaker: Unknown
en: That hit me right in the heart.
zh: 这直击我的内心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's so obvious just how personal and important this is to you.
zh: 很明显这对你来说是多么个人化和重要的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's clear you've just seen this happen again and again and you're just like-
zh: 很明显你只是看着这种情况一次又一次地发生，你只是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh yeah.
zh: 噢是啊。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been in the room where it happens.
zh: 我曾身临其境。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The proverbial room.
zh: 那个传说中的房间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's talk about what people should do and we'll poke around in different directions.
zh: 那么让我们来谈谈人们应该做什么，我们会从不同方向进行探讨。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the broad stroke solution to this?
zh: 针对这个的宏观解决方案是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what are some, say, three things, say an early stage founder should do this week, next week?
zh: 然后有哪些，比如说，三件事，比如说一个早期阶段的创始人在本周，下周应该做的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So broadly speaking, there is a blueprint.
zh: 所以从广义上讲，有一个蓝图。

speaker: Unknown
en: I promise when we talk about the horrors of living in late stage capitalism, it's easy to get depressed and just be like...
zh: 我保证当我们谈论生活在晚期资本主义中的恐怖时，很容易感到沮丧，然后就像……

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, literally part one of this book is called The Shape of the Abyss.
zh: 我是说，字面上这本书的第一部分就叫深渊的形状。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm not trying to sugarcoat it.
zh: 所以我不是想要粉饰太平。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is quite grim.
zh: 这是相当残酷的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you haven't studied these case studies, if you don't know the story of Whole Foods and what happened to it, if you don't know the story of Vectura, you don't know these stories, they're really grim and it's important to grapple with what happened so that it doesn't happen to you, so that you're prepared.
zh: 如果你没有研究过这些案例研究，如果你不知道全食超市发生了什么故事，如果你不知道 Vectura 的故事，你不知道这些故事，它们真的很残酷，重要的是要搞清楚发生了什么，这样它就不会发生在你身上，这样你就准备好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it can feel like we're so helpless.
zh: 但这可能会让我们感到如此无助。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as I said, this is a double mystery, not just why does this happen, but how can there be exceptions to a rule that's inevitable?
zh: 但正如我所说，这是一个双重谜团，不仅是为什么会发生这种情况，而且为什么在一个不可避免的规则中会有例外？

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me just tell one more story and then we'll get really into the tactical details because I want some stories that we can use.
zh: 所以让我再讲一个故事，然后我们真的进入战术细节，因为我想要一些我们可以使用的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's our fact set, our database to draw these principles out.
zh: 这是我们的事实集，我们的数据库，用来提取这些原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the blueprint, I'll give you the spoiler alert.
zh: 所以关于蓝图，我先给你剧透一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just mentioned before, ethos plus integrity, that's our formula.
zh: 我刚才提到过，精神加上正直，这是我们的公式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Ethos meaning internal alignment, character, choices, integrity, meaning the structure to resist to keep ourselves aligned with human flourishing.
zh: 精神意味着内在的一致性，性格，选择，正直，意味着这种结构来抵抗，以保持我们自己与人类繁荣相一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's go back in time a little further this time.
zh: 这次让我们把时间倒退得更久一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to go to 1920 Denmark.
zh: 我们要回到 1920 年的丹麦。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to tell you about a woman named Marie Krogh.
zh: 我想告诉你一个叫 Marie Krogh 的女人。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was one of the first doctors to practice in Denmark, like a credentialed doctor.
zh: 她是丹麦最早执业的医生之一，就像有执照的医生。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was a big advocate for women's medical education and she lived a very cool life in her own right.
zh: 她是女性医学教育的积极倡导者，她自己的生活也非常精彩。

speaker: Unknown
en: But she's famous today mostly because of her husband, August, who had just won the Nobel Prize, who was a very famous scientist.
zh: 但她今天出名主要是因为她的丈夫 August，他刚刚获得了诺贝尔奖，是一位非常著名的科学家。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she, unfortunately, right around the same time, got diagnosed with a fatal illness, an illness for which there was no known cure at that time.
zh: 而她，不幸的是，就在同一时间，被诊断出患有一种绝症，一种在当时没有已知疗法的疾病。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was called diabetes.
zh: 这叫糖尿病。

speaker: Unknown
en: So she gets this death sentence right as he's winning the Nobel Prize and he asks her, would she come with him to North America for his lecture tour anyway?
zh: 所以她接到了这个死亡判决，就在他获得诺贝尔奖的时候，他问她，无论如何她是否会陪他去北美进行巡回讲座？

speaker: Unknown
en: And she says yes.
zh: 她说好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the two of them travel to North America.
zh: 所以他们两个人一起去北美旅行。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's going around talking to scientists about his Nobel Prize.
zh: 他四处奔波与科学家谈论他的诺贝尔奖。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one night at dinner, Marie is sitting next to a scientist who tells her, "Hey, actually in Canada, there are these researchers who have figured out a new technology to isolate a substance called insulin, a potential cure for diabetes."
zh: 一天晚上在晚餐时，Marie 坐在一位科学家旁边，那位科学家告诉她，“嘿，其实在加拿大，有一些研究人员已经发明了一种新技术来分离一种叫做胰岛素的物质，这是一种潜在的糖尿病治愈方法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So Marie convinces her husband we should extend our trip, go to Canada, see this thing for themselves.
zh: 于是 Marie 说服她的丈夫我们应该延长行程，去加拿大，亲自去看看这个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: They do.
zh: 他们去了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They are both scientists, so they instantly understand the implications.
zh: 他们都是科学家，所以他们立刻理解了其中的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: This could not only save Marie's life, but millions more.
zh: 这不仅能挽救 Marie 的生命，还能挽救数百万人的生命。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they talked to the Canadians, "Could we commercialize this technology?"
zh: 于是他们与加拿大人交谈，“我们可以将这项技术商业化吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the Canadians said they're open to it, but everyone involved has a concern.
zh: 加拿大人说他们对此持开放态度，但每个参与者都有一个顾虑。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've been talking so far about the concern of being forced out of your company or having it taken away from you.
zh: 我们到目前为止谈论的是被迫离开你的公司或公司被夺走的顾虑。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is one danger, but there's also the temptation to harvest what others plant, the temptation to exploit, to extract instead of creating new value.
zh: 那是一种危险，但也存在收割他人劳动成果的诱惑，剥削的诱惑，掠夺而不是创造新价值的诱惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were worried about that.
zh: 他们担心那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now Lenny, I want you to imagine this scenario with me, okay?
zh: 现在 Lenny，我要你和我一起想象这个场景，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Imagine that I depend on you for a lifesaving cure.
zh: 想象我依赖你寻求救命疗法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have diabetes, you're the only maker of insulin in the world.
zh: 我患有糖尿病，你是世界上唯一的胰岛素制造商。

speaker: Unknown
en: In that case, I would want you to charge me a fair price.
zh: 在这种情况下，我希望你向我收取公平的价格。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very much so.
zh: 非常希望。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's both morally right, fair, but also I want you to have every incentive possible to keep making the drug, otherwise I might die.
zh: 这在道德上是正确的，公平的，而且我希望你拥有尽可能多的动力去继续生产这种药物，否则我可能会死。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But what if one day you wake up and you're like, "Wait a second, I don't have to charge Eric a fair price."
zh: 但如果有天你醒来，你想，“等一下，我不必向 Eric 收取公平的价格。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Or you have investors whispering in your ear, "Lenny, you don't have to charge him a fair price.
zh: 或者有投资者在你耳边低语，“Lenny，你不必向他收取公平的价格。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can charge him anything you want."
zh: 你可以向他收取任何你想要的价格。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what I would live in fear of.
zh: 那就是我会生活在恐惧中的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So decades before Martin Shkreli actually did this as a business strategy, the Kroghs and the Canadian scientists, they were worried that this might happen to their new company.
zh: 所以在 Martin Shkreli 实际将其作为商业策略实施的几十年前，Krogh 一家和加拿大科学家们，他们担心这可能会发生在他们的新公司身上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when they went back to Denmark to create this company, which they called the Nordisk Insulinlaboratorium, they incorporated it using this very particular structure.
zh: 所以当他们回到丹麦创建这家公司时，他们将其命名为 Nordisk Insulinlaboratorium，他们使用这种非常特殊的结构组建了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a for-profit company.
zh: 它是一家营利性公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: It does have outside investors, but it is owned, governed by a nonprofit foundation, a two-tiered structure that's called an industrial foundation in the literature.
zh: 它确实有外部投资者，但它由一家非营利基金会拥有和治理，这是一种双层结构，在文献中被称为工业基金会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if Nordisk Insulinlaboratorium sounds familiar, it should.
zh: 如果 Nordisk Insulinlaboratorium 听起来很耳熟，那应该是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the predecessor company to what we today call Novo Nordisk, one of the largest companies in the world.
zh: 这是我们要今天称之为 Novo Nordisk 的公司的前身，世界上最大的公司之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what's so interesting to me about this story is this structure has endured and protected the ethos of scientific integrity of Novo Nordisk for more than a hundred years.
zh: 这个故事对我来说最有趣的地方是，这种结构已经经受住考验并保护了 Novo Nordisk 的科学诚信精神一百多年。

speaker: Unknown
en: Meanwhile, so many of the best practices that your lawyers, your bankers, whoever advisors you have, they're going to be pushing best practices on you that are younger than the trees in your local park.
zh: 与此同时，你的律师、银行家、无论你有什么顾问，这么多最佳实践，他们会向你推行最佳实践，这些实践比你当地公园里的树还要年轻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what I want for everyone who's listening to this, founders, product managers, leaders, board members, I don't care.
zh: 所以我希望每一位正在听这个的人，创始人、产品经理、领导者、董事会成员，我不在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: The next time someone comes to you pushing a best practice and you're like, "Oh, I guess I have to do it this way because we live in an ROI dominated culture.
zh: 下次有人向你推行一种最佳实践，你想，“噢，我想我必须这样做，因为我们生活在一种由 ROI 主导的文化中。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to stack rank by ROI," or whatever the practice is, I want you to be like, well, that person sounds very smart.
zh: 我们必须按 ROI 进行堆栈排名，”或者无论那是什么实践，我希望你想，嗯，那个人听起来很聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're very well credentialed.
zh: 他们拥有很好的资历。

speaker: Unknown
en: They went to business school or whatever.
zh: 他们读过商学院或类似的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But ask yourself, are you sure they're smarter than a Nobel laureate?
zh: 但是问问你自己，你确定他们比一位诺贝尔奖得主更聪明吗？

zh: 一个拥有极其强大的技术、具有全球规模后果的时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot of founders, a lot of product people, they want to build a product that they can be proud of.
zh: 我认为许多创始人，许多产品人员，他们想构建一个他们引以为豪的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: That their grandkids will be happy to hear that that's how the family fortune was made.
zh: 他们的孙子辈会很高兴听到家族财富就是这样积累的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know a lot of people that are incredibly rich and so miserable.
zh: 我知道许多人非常富有却非常痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the thing, their baby, it got ruined, it got destroyed.
zh: 因为那个东西，他们的心血，被毁了，被摧毁了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why are we doing this?
zh: 我们为什么要这样做？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about what to actually do.
zh: 让我们谈谈实际上该做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is going to be a kind of a high level overview.
zh: 这将是一个高层次的概述。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously by the book, if you really want to get serious about this stuff coming out May 26th that all [inaudible 00:33:29] -
zh: 显然根据这本书，如果你真的想对这件事认真，5 月 26 日出版，所有 [听不清 00:33:29] ——

speaker: Unknown
en: May 26.

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, thank you.
zh: 是的，谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anywhere you buy books.
zh: 任何你买书的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what we should do.
zh: 谈谈我们应该做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just want to plant the seed of OpenAI is probably on people's minds as they hear this idea of building a nonprofit.
zh: 我只是想播下这颗种子，OpenAI 可能是人们在听到建立非营利组织这个想法时会想到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll get there.
zh: 我们会谈到那的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously in the book, I tell the Anthropic story where I played a bit part.
zh: 显然在书中,我讲述了 Anthropic 的故事,我在其中扮演了一个小角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we'll get to all that.
zh: 所以我们会谈到所有这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll get to all that.
zh: 我们会谈到所有这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: But let's start with the easy stuff first because AI is kind of like the humanities final exam.
zh: 但让我们先从简单的事情开始,因为 AI 有点像人文学科的期末考试。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've heard that joke.
zh: 你听过那个笑话。

speaker: Unknown
en: These issues are so amplified in AI, of course we have to get it right.
zh: 这些问题在 AI 中被极大地放大了,当然我们必须把它做对。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's incredibly important and I've done what I can to set us on that proper path.
zh: 我认为这非常重要,而且我已经尽我所能让我们走上那条正确的道路。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's easier to see it, I think, in simpler businesses.
zh: 但我认为,在更简单的业务中更容易看清这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: OpenAI's business is insanely complicated.
zh: OpenAI 的业务复杂得令人发指。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's look at it in some simpler examples first.
zh: 所以让我们先看一些更简单的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's start with the principle I call harder is easier.
zh: 让我们以我称之为“难就是易”的原则开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the leadership principle that anybody can adopt.
zh: 这是任何人都可以采用的领导原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nobody who's not ... You tell me you have no power whatsoever.
zh: 没有人不是……你告诉我你完全没有权力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's got some influence.
zh: 每个人都有一些影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you have any influence at all, you can adopt this principle.
zh: 如果你哪怕有一点点影响力,你就可以采用这个原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I talk to people about business, I don't care if I'm talking to two guys in a garage, founders, or I'm talking to super boards or CEO, C-suite, whatever.
zh: 当我和人们谈论业务时,我不在乎我是和车库里的两个人、创始人谈话,还是和超级董事会或 CEO、C 高管层谈话,无论什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone always says to me, just what you were saying a minute ago, it's like, "Man, Eric, business is already so hard.
zh: 每个人总是对我说,就像你一分钟前说的那样,就像是:“天啊,Eric,业务已经这么难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you want me to do extra?
zh: 现在你还想要我做额外的事情?

speaker: Unknown
en: Now I got to worry about the Vectura thing and this other stuff.
zh: 现在我还得担心 Vectura 这件事和其他东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Man, I'm already just trying to get through the day.
zh: 天啊,我只是想撑过这一天。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so hard."
zh: 太难了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think this is a basically backwards way of looking at it.
zh: 但我认为这是一种根本颠倒的看待方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I ask people, could it be, just consider the possibility that one of the reasons you're finding business so hard is that nobody trusts you.
zh: 因为我问人们,有没有可能,就考虑这种可能性,你发现业务如此困难的原因之一是没有人信任你。

speaker: Unknown
en: If they trusted you, maybe it would be a little bit easier.
zh: 如果他们信任你,也许会容易一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, actually, this is not a supposition, we have really good evidence.
zh: 实际上,这不是一个假设,我们有非常好的证据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Companies where their employees trust them, spend way less time on employee communications.
zh: 员工信任他们的公司,在员工沟通上花费的时间要少得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have much better alignment.
zh: 他们有更好的协同。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's kind of rowing in the same directions.
zh: 每个人都在朝同一个方向划船。

speaker: Unknown
en: When customers trust you, of course, you have way higher loyalty, your cost of customer acquisition is lower.
zh: 当客户信任你时,当然,你有更高的忠诚度,你的客户获取成本更低。

speaker: Unknown
en: But also customers more likely to stick with you after you make a mistake.
zh: 而且客户在你犯错后更有可能继续支持你。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're more likely to try your new product.
zh: 他们更有可能尝试你的新产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: So many people are like, "God, customers are so fickle.
zh: 很多人说:“天啊,客户太善变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have no loyalty.
zh: 他们没有忠诚度。

speaker: Unknown
en: They only care about the slick marketing from our competitors."
zh: 他们只关心我们竞争对手的那些花哨营销。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But maybe because you view these things as extra is part of the problem.
zh: 但也许因为你把这些事情看作额外的,本身就是问题的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So harder is easier is a principle that says, if you're willing to do the work upfront to commit to quality, to design, to ethics, to integrity, to safety, whatever the thing, I don't want to tell you what your values are, you tell me what they are.
zh: 所以“难就是易”是一个原则,如果你愿意预先做这项工作来致力于质量、设计、道德、正直、安全,无论是什么,我不想告诉你你的价值观是什么,你告诉我它们是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're willing to be principled in your decision making, you will get these unexpected rewards.
zh: 如果你愿意在你的决策中坚守原则,你将获得这些意想不到的回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, you can't do it for the reward or it doesn't work.
zh: 当然,你不能为了回报而做这件事,否则它就不起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to do it for the thing itself.
zh: 你必须为了事情本身而做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Trustworthiness is the most underrated asset in all of business.
zh: 可信度是所有商业中最被低估的资产。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the things that create trustworthiness, by definition, stack rank to the bottom if we do it by ROI.
zh: 而创造可信度的事情,根据定义,如果我们按 ROI 排序,它们会排在最底部。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because doing the right thing has intangible rewards, but tangible costs.
zh: 因为做正确的事有无形的回报,但有形的成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me give you another story.
zh: 所以让我给你讲另一个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because now we've been talking about lofty stuff, and I want to talk about something super practical.
zh: 因为现在我们一直在谈论高调的东西,我想谈谈超级实用的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I mentioned Cloudflare before.
zh: 我之前提到了 Cloudflare。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me actually tell the Cloudflare story.
zh: 所以让我实际上讲讲 Cloudflare 的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like Cloudflare because Matthew Prince and his co-founders, they were really anti-consulting BS talk.
zh: 我喜欢 Cloudflare,因为 Matthew Prince 和他的联合创始人,他们真的反咨询废话谈话。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the early days, they came out of Harvard Business School and so they were traumatized.
zh: 在早期,他们出自哈佛商学院,所以他们受到了创伤。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were just like, "No, I don't want to hear about mission statement.
zh: 他们就像是:“不,我不想听使命声明。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't want to hear about values.
zh: 我不想听价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are making a firewall and putting it in the cloud.
zh: 我们正在做一个防火墙并把它放到云端。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that complicated.
zh: 这没那么复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want to hear it."
zh: 我们不想听这些。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So for years, they had no mission statement.
zh: 所以多年来，他们没有使命宣言。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is a critical point.
zh: 这很关键。

speaker: Unknown
en: As leaders, we get so focused on value statement, mission statements.
zh: 作为领导者，我们太专注于价值宣言、使命宣言。

speaker: Unknown
en: We forget the mission statement is not the mission.
zh: 我们忘记了使命宣言不是使命。

speaker: Unknown
en: The map is not the territory.
zh: 地图不是疆域。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mission is an emergent property of the living superorganism of the thing we're birthing.
zh: 使命是我们正在孕育的这个生命超级有机体的涌现属性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not something you can slap on with a label.
zh: 这不是你可以贴个标签上去的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to build it in.
zh: 你必须把它构建进去。

speaker: Unknown
en: You want to have a company that stands for quality, you got to build quality in from the inside.
zh: 如果你想要一个代表质量的公司，你必须从内部把质量构建进去。

speaker: Unknown
en: Deming taught this in the 40s.
zh: Deming 在 40 年代就教过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not some new idea.
zh: 这不是什么新想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a critical idea.
zh: 这是一个关键的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what craftsmanship really means.
zh: 这才是工匠精神的真正含义。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we know that Cloudflare had a mission because quite often they would do this harder is easier stuff.
zh: 所以我们知道 Cloudflare 有使命，因为他们经常做这种“更难就是更容易”的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And without even really knowing why.
zh: 甚至不知道为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a very famous example in their history where pro-democracy protesters, I want to say ...
zh: 在他们的历史中有一个非常著名的例子，民主示威者，我想说……

speaker: Unknown
en: I forget what nation state they were pissing off.
zh: 我忘了他们惹恼了哪个民族国家。

speaker: Unknown
en: I better not say, in case I get it wrong.
zh: 我最好别说了，免得我说错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Pissing off some nation state, were having state-sponsored hackers trying to take their websites down so they couldn't coordinate their pro-democracy protests.
zh: 惹恼了某个民族国家，导致有国家支持的黑客试图让他们的网站下线，这样他们就无法协调他们的民主示威了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were going around Silicon Valley begging big tech companies to help them defend their websites.
zh: 他们在硅谷四处乞求大科技公司帮助他们保卫网站。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one would do it.
zh: 没人愿意这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: All these big mega companies, even Google were just like, "I'm too scared."
zh: 所有这些大公司，甚至 Google 都只是说，“我太害怕了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so Cloudflare, the tiny startup is like, "We'll do it."
zh: 于是 Cloudflare，这家小初创公司说，“我们要做。”

speaker: Unknown
en: These were free tier customers.
zh: 这些是免费层的客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: They weren't even paying any money.
zh: 他们甚至没付一分钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "Yes, we will incur the wrath of nation state level hackers to protect you because it's the right thing to do for no reward whatsoever."
zh: 他们就说，“是的，我们愿意招致国家级别黑客的愤怒来保护你们，因为这是正确的事情，没有任何回报。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That was just the kind of company they were.
zh: 这就是他们那类公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyway, a couple years in, they're having lunch and one of the engineers says, "You know why I like working at this company?
zh: 所以总之，几年后，他们在吃午饭，其中一个工程师说，“你知道我为什么喜欢在这家公司工作吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I just feel like it's the first place I've worked where we're trying to make a better internet."
zh: 我就是觉得这是我工作的第一个地方，我们在努力让互联网变得更好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And everyone on the table's like, "Yeah, make a better internet."
zh: 桌子上的每个人都像，“是啊，让互联网变得更好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone asked Matthew, "Oh, is that our mission statement?"
zh: 有人问 Matthew，“哦，那是我们的使命宣言吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Smack.
zh: 啪。

speaker: Unknown
en: "No, we don't have a mission statement.
zh: “不，我们没有使命宣言。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know what you're talking about?
zh: 我知道你在说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: No."

speaker: Unknown
en: But it actually was.
zh: 但实际上是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Over time, the engineers, people who worked there kept saying, "Make a better internet."
zh: 随着时间推移，在那里工作的工程师们一直说，“让互联网变得更好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That was how we talk about it.
zh: 这就是我们谈论它的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And eventually the founders had to be convinced, "Okay, let's adopt this as our mission statement," which they did.
zh: 最终创始人不得不被说服，“好吧，让我们采用这个作为我们的使命宣言，”他们确实这么做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's their mission statement to this day.
zh: 这就是他们今天的使命宣言。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had to eventually adopt formal values.
zh: 他们最终不得不采用正式的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you get bigger, these things really matter.
zh: 随着你变大，这些东西真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: It matters that you document those emergent properties, otherwise how are people supposed to know what they are?
zh: 记录这些涌现属性很重要，否则人们怎么知道它们是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Their number one value, by the way, is be principled, which is the ultimate harder is easier move.
zh: 顺便说一句，他们的首要价值观是有原则，这是极致的“更难就是更容易”的举措。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this all sounds great.
zh: 所以这听起来都很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's fun to have values.
zh: 拥有价值观很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's fun to have a mission statement when it doesn't cost you anything.
zh: 当不需要付出任何代价时，拥有一份使命宣言很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: But sometimes it can be very expensive.
zh: 但有时代价可能非常昂贵。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can make your life a lot harder.
zh: 它会让你的生活变得艰难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why we call the principle harder is easier.
zh: 这就是为什么我们称这一原则为“难即易”。

speaker: Unknown
en: One day, a junior engineer, not like some senior executive or anything, walks into Matthew Prince's office and he says, "Boss, isn't our mission statement to make a better internet?"
zh: 一天，一位初级工程师，不是什么高级高管之类的，走进 Matthew Prince 的办公室，他说，“老板，我们的使命宣言不是要让互联网更美好吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: When you're a CEO, any conversation that starts this way is definitely going to be extra work for you.
zh: 当你是 CEO 时，任何以这种方式开始的对话肯定都会给你带来额外的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're like, "What is it now?"
zh: 所以你会想，“这次又是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "Well, you were saying at the board meeting that our number one driver of revenue, the thing that causes people to upgrade from our free to premium plans," this is a few years ago now, "Is web encryption, SSL encryption.
zh: 就像是，“呃，你在董事会上说，我们的收入头号驱动因素，那个让人们从我们的免费计划升级到高级计划的东西，”这是几年前的事了，“是网页加密，SSL 加密。

speaker: Unknown
en: You said that makes sense because SSL encryption is expensive to offer, we can't offer it for free.
zh: 你说这很有道理，因为 SSL 加密提供起来很贵，我们无法免费提供。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to get the certificates and pay for them.
zh: 我们必须获取证书并为此付费。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to do all this extra cryptographic stuff as compute ... We have scarce compute, da-da-da-da-da."
zh: 我们必须做所有这些额外的加密计算工作…… 我们有稀缺的计算资源，啦啦啦啦啦。”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Sure."
zh: “当然。”

speaker: Unknown
en: "But boss, wouldn't a better internet be an encrypted internet?"
zh: “但是老板，更美好的互联网难道不应该是一个加密的互联网吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "Yes.
zh: 他就像，“是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what's your point?"
zh: 所以你的观点是？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So if it would be better, why are we giving it away for free?
zh: 所以如果那样会更好，为什么我们要免费赠送它？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so many people have heard this story.
zh: 很多人都听过这个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you've ever been a middle manager in a company, okay, you know this, you're just like, "Oh my God.
zh: 如果你在公司做过中层经理，好的，你懂这个，你就像，“天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every day someone walks into your office that's like, here you boss, let's give our product away for free for no reason."
zh: 每天都有人走进你的办公室说，给你老板，我们无缘无故把产品免费送人吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Your job as a middle manager is to be like, "No, we have a strategy.
zh: 作为中层经理，你的工作就是像，“不，我们有一个策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: Get back on the strategy.
zh: 回到策略上来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Redirect.
zh: 转向。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for your buy-in and your input, but we have a job to do."
zh: 感谢你的支持和投入，但我们有工作要做。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So everyone's expecting Matthew to react that way and would be the most normal thing in the world.
zh: 所以每个人都预期 Matthew 会那样反应，这本来是世界上最正常的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is our most profitable product and you're saying we should give it away for free, get lost.
zh: 这是我们最赚钱的产品，你说我们应该免费送人，滚蛋。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Matthew told me once I saw it, I couldn't unsee it.
zh: Matthew 曾告诉我，一旦我看到了它，我就无法视而不见。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he uttered the three keywords, he said, "Let's figure it out."
zh: 他说了三个关键词，他说，“让我们搞清楚它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Figure it out.
zh: 搞清楚它。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the leadership principle I think is so powerful, the figure it out principle.
zh: 我认为这是非常有力的领导原则，“搞清楚它”原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're committed to something, you stand for quality or whatever, it's going to make life harder.
zh: 如果你致力于某件事，你坚持质量或其他什么，这会让生活变得更艰难。

speaker: Unknown
en: The best leaders, the ones I really admire, they revel in it.
zh: 最好的领导者，那些我真正钦佩的人，他们会从中陶醉。

speaker: Unknown
en: They love the difficulty.
zh: 他们热爱这种困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because every time you have one of these impossible dilemmas, it's a chance to teach what you really stand for.
zh: 因为每当你要面对这些不可能的困境时，这都是一个教导你真正立场的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Matthew insisted the whole team rally to figure out how to give encryption away for free.
zh: 所以 Matthew 坚持整个团队团结起来搞清楚如何免费赠送加密。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now they couldn't just give it away for free.
zh: 现在他们不能直接免费赠送它。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Bankrupting the company won't achieve the mission."
zh: 他就像，“搞垮公司无法实现使命。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We had to find a way to make it sustainable.
zh: 我们必须找到一种方法使其可持续。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I won't go through all the technical details of how they managed to get the cost.
zh: 我不会详述他们如何降低成本的所有技术细节。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had to hand roll their own software and assembly language.
zh: 他们必须手工编写自己的软件和汇编语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had to do these complicated biz dev deals with certificate authorities.
zh: 他们必须与证书机构签署这些复杂的业务发展协议。

speaker: Unknown
en: They figured out how to do it and they drove their own cost down.
zh: 他们搞清楚了如何做到这一点，并降低了自己的成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, keep in mind that at any time ... First of all, I could have used the difficulty as an excuse, "Oh, it's too hard.
zh: 现在，请记住，在任何时候…… 首先，我本可以利用困难作为借口，“噢，这太难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can't do it."
zh: 我们做不到。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Give up.
zh: 放弃。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once they did it, they could have said, "Wait a minute, this is just free margin.
zh: 一旦他们做到了，他们可以说，“等一下，这只是免费的利润空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can make our costs lower and then we just get free money."
zh: 我们可以降低成本，然后我们就赚到了免费的钱。”

speaker: Unknown
en: When they shipped it, they had to report to their board on what happened.
zh: 当他们发布它时，他们必须向董事会报告发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: The conversion rates for premium product went down.
zh: 高级产品的转化率下降了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they could easily have bailed out at that point, but they didn't.
zh: 所以他们在那个时刻本可以轻易脱身，但他们没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: They stuck with it because this is what we stand for.
zh: 他们坚持了下来，因为这就是我们所代表的立场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, of course, it's a happy ending story.
zh: 当然，现在这是一个大团圆结局的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The top of funnel increased by an order of magnitude.
zh: 漏斗顶端增加了一个数量级。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, to this day, people still talk about how Cloudflare is like the reason you take for granted we have an encrypted internet.
zh: 事实上，直到今天，人们还在谈论 Cloudflare 就像是我们理所当然拥有加密互联网的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: The trust that they gained is the reason why they're a $70 billion company today.
zh: 他们获得的信任就是他们今天成为一家 700 亿美元公司的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: But most leaders, when asked to defend their principles, can't do it.
zh: 但大多数领导者，在被要求捍卫他们的原则时，都做不到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they've been taught ROI-based thinking, they've been taught shareholder primacy, they've been taught that that's the path of maximum profitability.
zh: 因为他们被灌输了基于 ROI 的思维，他们被灌输了股东至上主义，他们被灌输这才是利润最大化的路径。

speaker: Unknown
en: To give you an easy contrast, Andrew Mason, the founder of Groupon once told me this story.
zh: 为了给你一个简单的对比，Groupon 的创始人 Andrew Mason 曾经给我讲过这个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone remember Groupon?
zh: 大家都记得 Groupon 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe not now.
zh: 也许现在不记得了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's kind of fallen out of public consciousness, but there was this time where Groupon was one of the fastest growing private companies in America.
zh: 它已经有点淡出了公众意识，但曾经有一段时间 Groupon 是美国增长最快的私营公司之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was powered by a daily email with a cool deal.
zh: 它由一封附带优惠交易的每日邮件驱动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the whole thing was you got one email a day from Groupon.
zh: 整个事情就是你每天从 Groupon 收到一封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: They went public on the one email a day, that's how successful it was.
zh: 他们凭借每天一封邮件上市了，它是如此成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember he told me this story one day, his executives and employees started coming into his office and they'd be like, "You know, boss, we need to make the quarter.
zh: 我记得有一天他给我讲这个故事，他的高管和员工开始走进他的办公室，他们会说，“你知道，老板，我们需要完成季度目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to make more money.
zh: 我们需要赚更多的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're a public company now.
zh: 我们现在是一家上市公司了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want to be better than one email.
zh: 我们不想止步于一封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Have you considered two emails?"
zh: 你考虑过两封邮件吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was like, "No.
zh: 他就说，“不。

speaker: Unknown
en: One email a day," he's like, "That's our whole thing."
zh: 每天一封邮件，”他说，“这就是我们的全部。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But he said, over time, they ground him down and they kept saying, they were using language that sounds kind of lean start-upy.
zh: 但他说，随着时间的推移，他们磨垮了他，他们一直说，他们使用的那种听起来很像精益创业的语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Shouldn't we do an experiment?
zh: 我们不应该做个实验吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Shouldn't we look at the data?
zh: 我们不应该看看数据吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What about the IR ... it's just like, what about the ROI?
zh: IR 怎么说……这就像，ROI 怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so he's like, "All right, fine.
zh: 所以他就说，“好吧，行。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll run the experiment."
zh: 我们就做这个实验。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He ran the experiment.
zh: 他做了这个实验。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two emails a day makes more money.
zh: 每天两封邮件赚更多的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he couldn't say no.
zh: 所以他无法拒绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everything was fine for several months until someone came to his office and said, "You know, boss, you know what'd be better than two email?
zh: 一切都好过了几个月，直到有人来到他的办公室说，“你知道，老板，你知道什么比两封邮件更好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We should send three emails."
zh: 我们应该发三封邮件。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And next thing you know, they're sending eight emails.
zh: 你也知道接下来发生了什么，他们发八封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is not just Groupon.
zh: 这不仅仅是 Groupon。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have had so many CEOs tell me this exact story about email frequency.
zh: 有那么多 CEO 给我讲过关于邮件频率的完全相同的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Email frequency is like, for some reason, the tip of the spear where it's like, we don't really have any way to defend doing the right thing here.
zh: 邮件频率就像是，出于某种原因，是充当先锋的东西，就像是我们真的没有任何办法来捍卫在这里做正确的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we did the wrong thing.
zh: 所以我们做了错误的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: That destroyed the whole company.
zh: 那毁了整个公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: But in the short term, we made a bunch of money.
zh: 但在短期内，我们赚了一大笔钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you can stick to the harder is easier principle, that is the first line of defense against losing whatever it is that makes a company special.
zh: 所以如果你能坚持“更难即更容易”原则，那就是防止失去任何让公司变得特别的特质的第一道防线。

speaker: Unknown
en: Eric, you're such a wonderful storyteller.
zh: Eric，你真是一个很棒的讲故事的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just sitting here just compelled.
zh: 我只是坐在这里被深深吸引了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, thank you.
zh: 哦，谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am so excited to tell you about this season's supporting sponsor, Vanta.
zh: 我非常激动地要告诉你本季度的赞助商，Vanta。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta helps over 15,000 companies like Cursor, Ramp, Duolingo, Snowflake, and Atlassian earn and prove trust with their customers.
zh: Vanta 帮助超过 15,000 家公司，如 Cursor、Ramp、Duolingo、Snowflake 和 Atlassian，赢得并证明与客户的信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams are building and shipping products faster than ever thanks to AI.
zh: 团队正在以前所未有的速度构建和发布产品，这归功于 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as a result, the amount of risk being introduced into your product and your business is higher than it's ever been.
zh: 但结果是，引入到你的产品和你的业务中的风险量比以往任何时候都高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every security leader that I talk to is feeling the increasing weight of protecting their organization, their business, and not to mention, their customer data.
zh: 我交谈过的每一位安全领导者都感觉到保护他们的组织、他们的业务，更不用说他们的客户数据的压力越来越大。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because things are moving so fast, they are constantly reacting, having to guess at priorities, and having to make do with outdated solutions.
zh: 因为事物发展太快了，他们不断做出反应，不得不猜测优先级，不得不凑合使用过时的解决方案。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta automates compliance and risk management with over 35 security and privacy frameworks, including SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA.
zh: Vanta 自动化合规和风险管理，涵盖超过 35 个安全和隐私框架，包括 SOC 2、ISO 27001 和 HIPAA。

speaker: Unknown
en: This helps companies get compliant fast and stay compliant.
zh: 这有助于公司快速合规并保持合规。

speaker: Unknown
en: More than ever before, trust has the power to make or break your business.
zh: 比以往任何时候，信任更有能力成就或毁掉你的生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn more at vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 在 vanta.com/lenny 了解更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a listener of this podcast, you get $1,000 off Vanta.
zh: 作为本播客的听众，你可以获得 Vanta 1000 美元的优惠。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 那就是 vanta.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to talk through just like, what do you do?
zh: 我想聊聊就好比，你是做什么的？

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's write a mission statement.
zh: 所以有写一份使命声明。

speaker: Unknown
en: Values, is that a part of this?
zh: 价值观，这是其中的一部分吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just define your values.
zh: 只要定义你的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about the bullet points.
zh: 谈谈要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, yeah.
zh: 嗯，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, no.
zh: 所以再次，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Writing this statement is not valuable.
zh: 写这份声明是没有价值的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: The statement is not what it is.
zh: 声明并不是它的实质。

speaker: Unknown
en: The reason I use the old-fashioned word ethos.
zh: 我之所以使用老式词汇 ethos 的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tried to write this whole book without using any trendy consulting language at all.
zh: 我试图写完这本书，完全不使用任何时髦的咨询语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I try not to use the word stakeholder.
zh: 所以我尽量不使用 stakeholder 这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I try not to use the word culture.
zh: 我尽量不使用 culture 这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tried to really go old-fashioned.
zh: 我试图真正做到老派。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why I started with Stall Price.
zh: 这就是我从 Stall Price 开始的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rather than talk about stakeholders and mission statement and values, I want to know who are your fiduciaries?
zh: 而不是谈论利益相关者和使命声明和价值观，我想知道谁是你的受托人？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a real old-fashioned word, fiduciary.
zh: 就像真正的老式词汇，受托人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to me, the question every leader has to answer, and this is every product has to answer this question.
zh: 但对我来说，每个领导者必须回答的问题，这也是每个产品必须回答的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is its purpose?
zh: 它的目的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Who would you rather die than betray?
zh: 你宁愿死也不愿背叛谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you tell me, I want to have a high quality product.
zh: 所以如果你告诉我，我想要一个高质量的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would rather die than ship slot.
zh: 我宁愿死也不愿发货插槽。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about how Steve Jobs was.
zh: 想想 Steve Jobs 是怎样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a guy who would fight with people over the layout of the wires inside a computer he didn't want customers to be allowed to open and ever see.
zh: 这是一个会为了计算机内部电线布局而与人争执的人，而他不想让客户被允许打开并看到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's so classic, harder is easier, right?
zh: 这太经典了，更难就是更容易，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just it has to be a certain way.
zh: 就它必须是某种方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you know stories about Yvon Chouinard, the founder of Patagonia.
zh: 如果你知道关于 Patagonia 创始人 Yvon Chouinard 的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today he's more famous for his environmental activism, but he was a quality zealot.
zh: 如今他因环保活动而更出名，但他曾是一个质量狂热者。

speaker: Unknown
en: He believed that quality was an objective function and that every product had a quality level that it deserved.
zh: 他相信质量是一个目标函数，每个产品都有它应得的质量水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people think that's insane.
zh: 大多数人认为这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why Patagonia is such a success.
zh: 这就是 Patagonia 如此成功的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: That idea is powerful.
zh: 这个想法很强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: So whatever that thing is, that purpose.
zh: 所以无论那个东西是什么，那个目的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to find ways to encode it in our management system so that there's no way for us to make money by betraying the principle, whatever it is.
zh: 我们必须找到方法将其编码到我们的管理系统中，这样我们就无法通过背叛原则来赚钱，无论它是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this can happen both at the operational level and at the governance level.
zh: 所以这既可以发生在运营层面，也可以发生在治理层面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Both are really important.
zh: 两者都非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: People hearing this, I know some people are going to be like, "Purpose?
zh: 听到这个的人，我知道有些人会像，“目的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this some ESG nonsense?"
zh: 这是什么 ESG 废话吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a very funny quote in the book, Unilever, the big food giant.
zh: 书里有一个很好玩的引语，联合利华，这个食品巨头。

speaker: Unknown
en: We went through a phase a couple years ago where they were going to infuse purpose into all of their products.
zh: 我们在几年前经历了一个阶段，他们要将目的注入到他们的所有产品中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a Wall Street investor was just like, "I've had it with this."
zh: 一位华尔街投资者就只是说，“我已经受够了这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Wrote them a nasty letter, it was like, "Look at the point that we're debating the purpose of Hellman's mayonnaise, I think you've lost the plot."
zh: 给他们写了一封恶毒的信，信上说，“看看我们正在辩论 Hellman's 蛋黄酱用途这一点，我想你们已经迷失了方向。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love that quick because it's funny, but actually what's so funny about it to me is humble though it is, Hellman's mayonnaise is food.
zh: 我喜欢这个例子是因为它很有趣，但实际上对我来说，它真正有趣的地方在于，虽然 Hellman's 蛋黄酱很普通，但它是食物。

speaker: Unknown
en: Its purpose is super clear.
zh: 它的用途非常明确。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, if you think it doesn't matter what the product manager who runs Hellman's mayonnaise thinks the purpose is, going back to our vital farms question, think how easy it would be when an efficiency consultant shows up and says, "I think we could save three cents on the bill of materials if you just make the thing carcinogenic."
zh: 还有，如果你认为负责 Hellman's 蛋黄酱的产品经理认为的用途是什么并不重要，回到我们关于 Vital Farms 的问题，想一想当一位效率顾问出现时说，“我认为如果你把东西做得致癌，我们可以在物料清单上省下三分钱”，这会是多么容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're like, "Oh, wouldn't that come back to bite us?"
zh: 然后你会说，“噢，那不会反过来咬我们一口吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Yeah, but long after your stock options are vested, buddy, what do you care?"
zh: “是的，但在你的股票期权归属之后很久，伙计，你关心什么呢？”

speaker: Unknown
en: If the product manager thinks their purpose is quality, that matters.
zh: 如果产品经理认为他们的用途是质量，那很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: If they think their purpose is extraction and exploitation, it matters.
zh: 如果他们认为他们的用途是榨取和剥削，那很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, if you think I'm exaggerating, the product managers at Johnson & Johnson put asbestos in the baby powder and covered it up.
zh: 再说一次，如果你认为我在夸大其词，强生公司的产品经理在婴儿爽身粉里添加了石棉并掩盖了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because although they said their purpose, their mission statement was patient health.
zh: 因为虽然他们说他们的用途，他们的使命宣言是患者健康。

speaker: Unknown
en: Their actual mission had become growth, optimization, quarterly targets.
zh: 他们实际的使命已经变成了增长、优化、季度目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what we want to do is that's the first most important technique is like, what is the purpose?
zh: 所以我们要做的是，那是第一个也是最重要的技巧，就是，什么是用途？

speaker: Unknown
en: Who would we rather die than betray?
zh: 我们宁愿死也不愿背叛谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to write it down.
zh: 你必须把它写下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to say, as Saul Price did, customers first, employees second, shareholders last.
zh: 你必须说，就像 Saul Price 所说的那样，客户第一，员工第二，股东最后。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or the great Peter Drucker said it with actually, he said that that's backwards.
zh: 或者伟大的 Peter Drucker 实际上说过，他说那是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It should be employees first, customer second, shareholders last.
zh: 应该是员工第一，客户第二，股东最后。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't care.
zh: 我不在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: You tell me what you believe, you got to write it down.
zh: 你告诉我你相信什么，你必须把它写下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we have to do a thing I call mission drive.
zh: 然后我们必须做一件事，我称之为使命驱动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Companies that claim to be mission driven, most of them are just mission hopeful.
zh: 那些声称是使命驱动的公司，它们中的大多数都只是对使命抱有希望。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's bullshit.
zh: 那都是废话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like candy coating on top of an extractive engine.
zh: 那就像是包裹在榨取引擎之上的糖衣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sorry, I don't buy it.
zh: 抱歉，我不买账。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're serious about being mission driven, you have to show me that you cannot profit except by achieving the mission.
zh: 如果你真的对使命驱动很认真，你必须向我表明，除非实现使命，否则你就无法盈利。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the audit we have to do.
zh: 那就是我们必须进行的审计。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to look at, if someone in the company got tempted to cut quality, to cut corners, to decrease performance, to deal with ...
zh: 我们必须审视，如果公司里有人受到诱惑去降低质量、偷工减料、降低性能、去应对……

speaker: Unknown
en: The things that tend to get cut first, safety, performance, quality, design, those are always the most vulnerable.
zh: 那些倾向于最先被削减的东西：安全、性能、质量、设计，这些总是最脆弱的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Innovation, I guess is fifth.
zh: 创新，我想是第五个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's like the five horsemen of the apocalypse is like, you can get rid of those things and nothing bad happens right away.
zh: 所以这就像是启示录中的天启四骑士，意思就是，你可以摆脱那些东西，而且不会立刻发生任何坏事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the whole point of trust is I can betray you and you wouldn't even notice.
zh: 因为信任的整个重点在于，我可以背叛你，而你甚至不会注意到。

speaker: Eric Ries
en: So we have-
zh: 所以我们必须——

speaker: Unknown
en: ...point of trust is I can betray you and you wouldn't even notice.
zh: ……信任的重点在于我可以背叛你，而你甚至不会注意到。

speaker: Unknown
en: So those are like the canary in the coal mine.
zh: 所以那些就像是煤矿里的金丝雀。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is anyone's bonus target?
zh: 有任何人的奖金目标吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is our OKR system?
zh: 我们的 OKR 系统呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anyone in this team?
zh: 这个团队里有任何人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, you could do this at the company level, of course, but you're just a team, a team of five.
zh: 而且，你当然可以在公司层面做这件事，但你只是一个小团队，五个人的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anyone on this team who could profit by portraying one of our principles?
zh: 这个团队里有人能通过违背我们的原则之一来获利吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it possible?
zh: 这可能吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's really possible.
zh: 这是真的可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is actually why automated testing is so good.
zh: 这实际上就是为什么自动化测试如此之好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about how many people can just break the design no one even notices.
zh: 想想有多少人可以破坏设计而甚至没人注意到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, it's a test notice.
zh: 嗯，有测试会注意到。

speaker: Unknown
en: With AI, we have so many new capabilities for auditing, for preventing, for making sure, but we have to choose to use the tools for that.
zh: 有了 AI，我们拥有如此多的新能力来进行审计、预防、确保，但我们必须选择为此使用这些工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's one.
zh: 这是第一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's the general category of things that we can build.
zh: 所以那是我们可以构建的事物的一般类别。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so just to make sure it's clear, so it's write down your purpose, whatever that is.
zh: 所以只是为了确保清楚，就是写下你的用途，无论那是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I imagine it's like a sentence is the idea.
zh: 我想象这就好比一句话就是那个点子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that'd be great.
zh: 是的，那会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: As simple as better.
zh: 越简单越好。

speaker: Unknown
en: To make a better internet is such a great purpose.
zh: 去打造一个更好的互联网，这是如此伟大的宗旨。

speaker: Unknown
en: I tell in the story, in the book, the story of Devoted Health, they're a health insurance company, but their instruction to their employees is to treat every customer the way you would your own parents.
zh: 我在故事里，在书里，讲了 Devoted Health 的故事，他们是一家健康保险公司，但他们给员工的指示是，像对待你自己的父母那样对待每一位顾客。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've heard that one.
zh: 我听说过那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just so easy and simple and clear to understand.
zh: 它是如此简单、清晰且易懂。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's write down your purpose.
zh: 所以就是写下你的宗旨。

speaker: Unknown
en: Describe your mission and more importantly, the mission.
zh: 描述你的使命，更重要的是，那个使命。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does it drive the mission?
zh: 是什么驱动着使命？

speaker: Unknown
en: The mission-
zh: 使命驱动，使命驱动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mission drive, mission drive.
zh: 使命驱动，使命驱动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mission drive, which basically... Is there a structural way to do this or is this just go through everything going on and see is there-
zh: 使命驱动，这基本上……是有结构化的方法来做这件事，还是这就只是把正在发生的每件事过一遍然后看看是否有——

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I talk about it in a more formal way in the book, but basically I would say that it is to identify your fiduciary commitments.
zh: 我是说，我在书里用更正式的方式谈论了它，但基本上我想说，那就是去确定你的信托承诺。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who are the people that you're trying to commit to do something good for?
zh: 那些你试图承诺为其做些好事的人是谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the metrics or targets that you want to have for each of those people?
zh: 你想要为这些人设定什么样的指标或目标？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what is the system, the accountability system to make sure that those commitments are as important to you as making money?
zh: 然后什么是系统，那个问责系统，以确保这些承诺对你来说和赚钱一样重要？

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's essentially these kind of like OKRs for your stakeholder.
zh: 所以这基本上就是那种像是为你利益相关者的 OKRs。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's not call them stakeholders.
zh: 我们别叫他们利益相关者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see how hard it is to do this.
zh: 我明白做这件事有多难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's exactly right.
zh: 是的，完全正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like OKRs for the... And I just, again, people always, when we start using this language, people are like, "You're trying to impose your values on me."
zh: 这就像是给……的 OKRs，而且我，再说一次，人们总是，当我们开始使用这种语言时，人们就会说，“你在试图把你的价值观强加给我。”

speaker: Unknown
en: No, you tell me what you care about and then you tell me how you're measuring the things you claim to care about.
zh: 不，你告诉我你在乎什么，然后你告诉我你如何衡量那些你声称在乎的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the book, I have a section called Don't Be Evil versus the Quarterly Report.
zh: 在书里，我有一节叫作「不作恶」与季度报告。

speaker: Unknown
en: I made a study of the blog posts that longtime Google employees write when they leave Google.
zh: 我对那些长期 Google 员工离职时写的博客文章做了一个研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a genre.
zh: 这就像是一个流派。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's 50 of them.
zh: 大约有 50 篇。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're incredible documents actually.
zh: 它们其实是非常棒的文件。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you've never read them, they're all linked in the book.
zh: 如果你从没读过，它们都链接在书里。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can read them yourself.
zh: 你可以自己去读。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they all have this very wistful, sad quality to them because something really...
zh: 它们都带有这种非常惆怅、悲伤的特质，因为有些东西真的……

speaker: Unknown
en: And listen, for the record, Google's a great company.
zh: 听着，声明一下，Google 是一家很棒的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not saying Google's bad, but Google used to have this don't be evil ethos, and it got lost.
zh: 我不是说 Google 不好，但 Google 曾经有这种「不作恶」的精神，但它丢失了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It got taken off the website.
zh: 它从网站上被撤下了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then it showed up in the employee handbook.
zh: 然后它出现在了员工手册里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it's not even in the employee handbook anymore.
zh: 现在它甚至都不在员工手册里了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, Google's been sued twice now for breaking the don't be evil pledge, and they've had to settle both lawsuits.
zh: 事实上，Google 现在已经两次因为违反「不作恶」誓言而被起诉，并且他们不得不和解这两起诉讼。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how sad the situation has become at Google.
zh: 这就是 Google 的处境变得多么令人悲伤。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember talking to one of these ex Googlers, he'd been there I think 13 years.
zh: 我记得和其中一位前 Google 人聊过，他在那儿待了大概 13 年。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I said to him, look, he was like, "Why did we lose it when we had such good intentions?
zh: 我对他说，你看，他当时说，“为什么当我们有着如此良好的初衷时，却把它弄丢了？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I wanted to believe management."
zh: 我本来是想相信管理层的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "I liked management.
zh: 他当时说，“我喜欢管理层。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I thought they had their heart in the right place."
zh: 我以为他们是出于好意。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet, I said, "Okay, answer me this hypothetical just real quick.
zh: 然而我说，“好的，快速回答我这个假设性问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is the probability that Google will file its next quarterly report on time?"
zh: Google 按时提交下一份季度报告的概率是多少？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He was like, "That's a dumb question, Eric.
zh: 他就像说，“那是个愚蠢的问题，Eric。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously they're going to do it."
zh: 很明显他们会这么做。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "No, but give it a number, probability."
zh: 我就像说，“不，但给它一个数字，概率。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, 100%.
zh: 他就像说，100%。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it literally 100.0?
zh: 字面上就是 100.0 吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: As certain as the sun will rise tomorrow?
zh: 像明天太阳会升起一样确定吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, great.
zh: 我说，很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now tell me the probability that Google might accidentally kill somebody and cover it up.
zh: 现在告诉我 Google 可能意外致人死亡并掩盖此事的概率。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was like, "Come on, man.
zh: 他就像说，“得了吧，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: They probably wouldn't do that.
zh: 他们大概不会那么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not fair.
zh: 那不公平。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Put a number on it.
zh: 给它一个数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it 100%?"

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Well, 90%, 95%."

speaker: Unknown
en: And you could see he was like, "Well, they got sued for this."
zh: 你能看到他就像说，“嗯，他们因为这个被起诉过。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "What if the self-driving car hit somebody?"
zh: 他就像说，“如果自动驾驶汽车撞了人怎么办？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He could already think of the way it could happen.
zh: 他已经能想到它可能发生的方式了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Okay, how can it be that quarterly reporting is the thing that we're sure of?"
zh: 我说，“好吧，怎么可能季度报告是我们确定的事情？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And manslaughter, we think are human beings confused.
zh: 而过失杀人，我们认为人类是困惑的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They love quarterly reports and they're not sure about manslaughter?
zh: 他们喜欢季度报告，却对过失杀人不确定？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, come on.
zh: 不，得了吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is going on here?
zh: 这里到底发生了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Google report, it's quarterly report on time because there is a massive, unbelievably expensive apparatus to make sure it happens every time.
zh: Google 报告，它按时发布季度报告是因为有一个庞大的、难以置信的昂贵机制来确保它每次都发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And don't be evil was just a slogan.
zh: 而‘不作恶’只是一个口号。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if someone tells you, "I'm serious about something," great.
zh: 所以如果有人告诉你，“我对某事是认真的，”很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now show me the apparatus.
zh: 现在给我看看那个机制。

speaker: Unknown
en: Show me the commitments you've made to make sure that happens every time, no exceptions.
zh: 向我展示你为确保每次都发生而做出的承诺，没有例外。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you don't have one, then they are lying to you no matter how good their intentions are.
zh: 如果你没有，那么他们就是在对你撒谎，无论他们的初衷有多好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this might be a good segue to the second bucket of stuff.
zh: 这可能是过渡到第二类东西的一个好引子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the way you described it earlier, I think is a really helpful way of framing it.
zh: 所以你之前描述它的方式，我认为是一个非常有用的框架方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the ethos, which is what we've been talking about, values, purpose, mission, and then there's integrity.
zh: 有精神气质，这是我们在谈论的，价值观、目的、使命，然后是正直。

speaker: Unknown
en: So talk about that.
zh: 所以谈谈那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why did I use the word integrity?
zh: 那么我为什么用了正直这个词？

speaker: Unknown
en: In normal human language, not consultant speak, but among normal people, integrity has two meanings.
zh: 在正常的人类语言中，不是咨询术语，而是在普通人中，正直有两种含义。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is like, Lenny, you're a very high integrity person.
zh: 一种是像，Lenny，你是一个非常正直的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you say you're going to meet me at two o'clock at a place, I know you're going to be there.
zh: 如果你说你会两点钟在一个地方见我，我知道你会到那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's got a friend like that where if they say they're going to do something, they're going to do it.
zh: 每个人都有这样的朋友，如果他们说他们会做某事，他们就会去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And more importantly, if they're not sure, if they're like, "I think I can be there at two o'clock," they never would say, "I'll see you at two o'clock."
zh: 更重要的是，如果他们不确定，如果他们像说，“我想我两点钟能在那里，”他们绝不会说，“两点钟见。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They won't do it.
zh: 他们不会这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: They know that to tell the truth requires you to really make a commitment.
zh: 他们知道说真话要求你真正做出承诺。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's the ability to keep your promise.
zh: 所以这就是信守诺言的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we also have the word structural integrity.
zh: 但我们也有结构完整性这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I told them before, the difference between corroded bolts and stainless steel.
zh: 我之前告诉过他们，生锈的螺栓和不锈钢之间的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: Organizations, these two senses of the word become one.
zh: 在组织中，这个词的这两种含义合二为一。

speaker: Unknown
en: An organization that is weak cannot keep its promises because the person making the promise won't be there.
zh: 一个软弱的组织无法信守承诺，因为做出承诺的人不会在那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Imagine you got a promise from the founders of Vectura that they'll never sell cigarettes to children.
zh: 想象一下你得到了 Vectura 创始人的承诺，他们永远不会把香烟卖给孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oops, sorry.
zh: 哎呀，抱歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, that's not integrity.
zh: 不，那不是正直。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the goal of structural integrity for an organization is to give it the power to resist temptation from the inside, resist betrayal at the board level and resist pressure from the outside.
zh: 因此，为企业保持结构正直的目标是赋予它抵御内部诱惑、抵御董事会层面的背叛以及抵御外部压力的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: As I mentioned, Costco very famously has this governance fortress that protects it from outside pressure.
zh: 正如我提到的，Costco 著名地拥有这个治理堡垒，保护它免受外部压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people have come for Costco.
zh: 人们已经找上 Costco 了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I quote some hilarious quotes in the book about one of my favorites is Costco takes money that rightfully belongs to shareholders and instead invest it in improving the customer experience.
zh: 我在书中引用了一些搞笑的引语，其中我最喜欢的一句是 Costco 拿走了本应属于股东的钱，转而投资于改善客户体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's meant to be a criticism.
zh: 这本意是一种批评。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not what they're supposed to do.
zh: 那不是他们应该做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why can they resist?
zh: 那为什么他们能抵抗呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they have the tools they need to fight back.
zh: 因为他们拥有反击所需的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Their board seeds its responsibility as a bulwark against pressure rather than as an amplifier of financial gravity.
zh: 他们的董事会将其责任视为抵御压力的堡垒，而不是金融引力的放大器。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that can sound very challenging and abstract as now we're talking about board level stuff, IPOs, people, a lot of people are like, "Oh, I don't have the power for that."
zh: 所以这听起来可能非常有挑战性且很抽象，因为现在我们在谈论董事会层面的东西、IPO，很多人就像，“噢，我没有那个权力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, that's okay.
zh: 好的，没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can start with something very simple.
zh: 我们可以从一些非常简单的事情开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: We started with purpose and ethos.
zh: 我们从宗旨和特质开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can start with purpose here too.
zh: 我们也可以从这里的宗旨开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mentioned before that most founders have never read their own corporate charter, which if you're a founder and you're listening to this and you've never read your own corporate charter, you have a company that is operating, it is your homework.
zh: 我之前提到过，大多数创始人从未阅读过他们自己的公司章程，如果你是创始人并且你在听这个，而且你从未阅读过你自己的公司章程，你有一家正在运营的公司，这是你的家庭作业。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to do this.
zh: 你必须做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to know what it says.
zh: 你必须知道上面写了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is so common that the HBO show Silicon Valley makes a joke about it where one of the investors is upgrading the founder to be like, "You don't know how your own effing company works?"
zh: 这太普遍了，以至于 HBO 的剧集《硅谷》为此开了个玩笑，其中一个投资者把创始人升职了，就像，“你不知道你自己该死的公司是怎么运作的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because he loses control of it.
zh: 因为他失去了对它的控制。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they did their homework.
zh: 所以他们做了他们的家庭作业。

speaker: Unknown
en: This really does happen.
zh: 这真的发生了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you do read your charter, you probably will be more confused than you are now because you will read a sentence like this.
zh: 但是如果你确实读了你的章程，你可能会比现在更困惑，因为你会读到像这样的一句话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It will say:
zh: 它会说：

speaker: Unknown
en: "The Acme Corporation is hereby incorporated to pursue any lawful act or activity."
zh: “Acme Corporation 特此成立，以追求任何合法的行为或活动。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you read that and you're like, "That sounds pretty open-ended."
zh: 你读了那个，你会想，“这听起来非常开放。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Wrong.
zh: 错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It sounds open-ended, but it's not.
zh: 听起来很开放，但事实并非如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Unfortunately, we live in the era of what's called shareholder primacy, meaning that according to this theory, which is the governing theory of our lives, we live under this law today, right now.
zh: 不幸的是，我们生活在一个所谓的股东至上的时代，这意味着根据这个理论，这是我们生活的指导理论，我们今天受制于这条法律，就在此刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: This says that an organization is not a vital, beautiful, living thing.
zh: 这表明一个组织不是一个充满活力的、美丽的、有生命力的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rather, it is a financial instrument designed to enrich shareholders and nothing else.
zh: 相反，它是旨在让股东致富的金融工具，除此之外别无其他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And therefore, any lawful act or activity today means maximize shareholder returns under the law.
zh: 因此，今天的任何合法行为或活动意味着在法律下最大化股东回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: People have been raised... Now we're old enough.
zh: 人们被教育……现在我们足够老了。

speaker: Unknown
en: This idea is old enough that we now have a generation of people who've been raised as if this was a natural law.
zh: 这个观念已经足够老了，以至于我们现在有一代人被教育成好像这是一条自然法则。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is how capitalism has always been, but that's wrong.
zh: 资本主义一直都是这样的，但那是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: For the vast majority of the time, for hundreds of years, we have had joint stock corporations.
zh: 在绝大多数时间里，在几百年里，我们一直有股份公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Only the last 40 have we had this idea.
zh: 只有过去 40 年我们才有这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before the '80s, it was considered obvious.
zh: 在 80 年代之前，这被认为是显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our grandparents thought it was obvious.
zh: 我们的祖父母认为这是显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our great-grandparents thought it was obvious.
zh: 我们的曾祖父母认为这是显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Adam Smith thought it was obvious.
zh: 亚当·斯密认为这是显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone before thought it was obvious that corporations existed to pursue a specific thing, what's called a beneficial purpose in the law.
zh: 之前的每个人都认为这是显而易见的，即公司的存在是为了追求特定的事情，在法律中被称为有益目的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So in the 19th century, for example, if you wanted to make a company, you had to make a declaration to your state legislature that this thing you wanted to make would do something that is publicly beneficial.
zh: 所以例如在 19 世纪，如果你想创办一家公司，你必须向你的州立法机构发表声明，表明你想制造的这个东西会做一些对公众有益的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'd be like, "I want to make a railroad."
zh: 你会像，“我想修一条铁路。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They'd be like, "Why?"
zh: 他们会像，“为什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You had to say it would be beneficial to the public to have a canal between this place and that place.
zh: 你必须说在这个地方和那个地方之间修一条运河对公众是有益的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just to get a sense of how different our best practices are than the historical norms in the 19th century, let's say you were the richest person in America and you're like, "I want to buy this company and change what it does because I can."
zh: 为了感受一下我们的最佳实践与 19 世纪的历史规范有多么不同，假设你是美国最富有的人，你就像，“我想买下这家公司并改变它的所作所为，因为我可以。”

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, the board would be authorized to fight you to the death.
zh: 首先，董事会将被授权与你抗争到底。

speaker: Unknown
en: They didn't have to say yes.
zh: 他们不必说同意。

speaker: Unknown
en: They would do crazy stuff in the night.
zh: 他们会在夜里做一些疯狂的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: These battles were legendary.
zh: 这些战斗是传奇性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of them are hilarious what would go on between these people.
zh: 其中有些很滑稽，看这些人之间发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the second thing, let's say you succeeded anyway.
zh: 但第二件事，假设你无论如何都成功了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You took the company over and you said, "I'm going to change its purpose from make a railroad to maximize shareholder value."
zh: 你接管了公司，并说，「我要改变它的宗旨，从经营铁路转变为最大化股东价值。」

speaker: Unknown
en: That would have been a crime.
zh: 那本会是一种犯罪。

speaker: Unknown
en: The courts would void your charter.
zh: 法院会废除你的章程。

speaker: Unknown
en: You would earn the corporate death penalty for having exceeded the authority of your corporation.
zh: 你会因越权而招致公司的死刑。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is a new idea that we live under.
zh: 所以这是我们生活在其下的一个新观念。

speaker: Unknown
en: We think it's natural, but it's very new.
zh: 我们认为它是自然的，但它非常新。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you don't want that, and that's what doomed Vectura and all these companies, if you don't want that, you can change it.
zh: 所以如果你不想要那样，这也是导致 Vectura 和所有这些公司毁灭的原因，如果你不想要那样，你可以改变它。

speaker: Unknown
en: The good news for you listening right now in the year 2026 is that a band of corporate governance rebels have spent the last 15 or 20 years fighting this and building alternative structures that are available to you.
zh: 对于现在 2026 年正在收听的你来说，好消息是，一群公司治理叛逆者在过去 15 到 20 年里一直在对抗这一点并构建可供你使用的替代结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of them is called the Public Benefit Corporation or PBC.
zh: 其中一种称为公益公司或 PBC。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of confusion about this out there because people have seen the little B with a circle on it at their farmer's market.
zh: 对此有很多困惑，因为人们在农贸市场见过那个带圈的小 B。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that.
zh: 不是那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's confusing because the same people invented that who also invented this and the word B, the letter B is in both.
zh: 这很令人困惑，因为发明那个东西的人也发明了这个，而且单词 B、字母 B 在两者里都有。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I get it's confusing.
zh: 所以我知道这很令人困惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: But no, Public Benefit Corp is the easiest thing I will tell you to do on this whole podcast.
zh: 但不，公益公司是我在整个播客中会告诉你的最简单的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could not be easier.
zh: 不可能更简单了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a two-page legal filing that you just submit.
zh: 它是一份只有两页的法律文件，你只需要提交它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your lawyers can submit it for you in Delaware tomorrow.
zh: 你的律师明天就可以在特拉华州为你提交它。

speaker: Unknown
