en: You just say, "This is the purpose of this company, not any lawful act or purpose."
zh: 你只需要说，「这是公司的宗旨，而不是任何合法行为或目的。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And said, "No, this is a company designed to advance human flourishing by creating safe and responsible AI systems.
zh: 并说，「不，这家公司旨在通过创造安全且负责任的 AI 系统来促进人类繁荣。

speaker: Unknown
en: We advance human flourishing by creating high quality products and selling them, whatever."
zh: 我们通过创造高质量产品并销售它们来促进人类繁荣，诸如此类。」

speaker: Unknown
en: Whatever you do, just write it down.
zh: 无论你做什么，写下来就好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It couldn't be any easier.
zh: 不可能更容易了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And most of the best companies today are using this structure, like all the major AI labs are incorporated as PBCs, Anthropic most famously of all.
zh: 而且今天大多数最优秀的公司都在使用这种结构，就像所有主要的 AI 实验室都注册为 PBC，最著名的莫过于 Anthropic。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't guarantee that you're the good guys.
zh: 它不保证你们是好人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just writing it down doesn't do that much, but it does solve one very specific problem, which is if someone one day sues you saying you breached your fiduciary duty to investors, you could say, "Nope, investors agreed that this is our purpose to do this thing."
zh: 仅仅写下来并不能起那么大作用，但它确实解决了一个非常具体的问题，就是如果有一天有人起诉你，说你违背了对投资者的信托责任，你可以说，「不，投资者同意这就是我们要做这件事的宗旨。」

speaker: Unknown
en: Why has Anthropic been able to resist all this pressure?
zh: 为什么 Anthropic 能够抵抗所有这些压力？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is one of several of the interlocking components of the armor that protects Anthropic from outside pressure.
zh: 这是保护 Anthropic 免受外部压力的相互锁定的盔甲组件之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you don't do this, I can't help you, man.
zh: 如果你不做这个，我帮不了你，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the very minimum.
zh: 这是最低限度。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to do at least this.
zh: 你至少得做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, if you're not at the founder, you probably are wondering, have we done this at my company?
zh: 现在，如果你不是创始人，你可能想知道，我们在我的公司做过这个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is going to sound so dumb, I know, but you can just ask.
zh: 这听起来会很蠢，我知道，但你完全可以问。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if you're in a job interview, I remember someone once came to me and asked me for advice.
zh: 即使你在求职面试中，我记得曾经有人来找我要建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: They wanted to advocate for these ideas, but they were like, "I need a job.
zh: 他们想倡导这些想法，但他们像是，「我需要一份工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't even work anywhere yet."
zh: 我甚至还没在什么地方工作。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And they were like, "Caveat."
zh: 而且他们像是，「警告。」

speaker: Unknown
en: I was about to give them some advice and before you give me your advice, you need to know that I'm not courageous.
zh: 我正要给他们一些建议，在你给我你的建议之前，你需要知道我不勇敢。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I can't do anything scary.
zh: 所以我不能做任何可怕的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not willing to be an activist.
zh: 我不愿意做一个活动家。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just need a job, man, but I am willing to do something.
zh: 我只是需要一份工作，伙计，但我愿意做点什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I felt like they'd asked me for a no bake cookie recipe.
zh: 但我觉得他们要的是一个免烤饼干食谱。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a no courage.
zh: 就像是一个无需勇气。

speaker: Unknown
en: What can I do?
zh: 我能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "Okay."
zh: 然后我说，「好的。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can do this if you're in a job interview or just ask your boss either way.
zh: 如果你在求职面试中你可以这样做，或者不管怎样直接问你的老板。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then at the end of your interview, we're going to ask you, do you have any other questions?
zh: 然后在面试结束时，我们会问你，你还有其他问题吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You can say, "Is this a mission driven company?"
zh: 你可以说，“这是一家使命驱动的公司吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: They're going to say yes.
zh: 他们会说是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, but how do you know?
zh: 好的，但你怎么知道？

speaker: Unknown
en: What kind of things do we do that are mission driven?
zh: 我们做哪些事情是使命驱动的？

speaker: Unknown
en: They'd be, "Oh, we have free beer on Fridays and we clean up the local park on Saturday."
zh: 他们会说，“噢，我们周五有免费啤酒，周六我们会打扫当地的公园。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know what they're going to say.
zh: 我不知道他们会说什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe something great, maybe something...
zh: 也许是些很棒的事情，也许是些什么……

speaker: Unknown
en: But whatever they say, just nod and be like, "Great."
zh: 但无论他们说什幺，只要点头并说，“太棒了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And just ask.
zh: 然后就问。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cool.
zh: 很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the legal mission also?
zh: 这也是法律上的使命吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that in the charter?
zh: 这在章程里吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Almost certainly the person you're asking this question to is not going to know the answer to your question, but it's a legitimate question because if you're working in a company that doesn't have this in the charter, you're going to be betrayed eventually.
zh: 几乎可以肯定，你问这个问题的对象不会知道答案，但这是一个合理的问题，因为如果你在一个没有将其写入章程的公司工作，你最终会被背叛。

speaker: Unknown
en: You deserve to know.
zh: 你有权知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: But more importantly, just by asking, you've now forced them to get the answer.
zh: 但更重要的是，仅仅通过询问，你现在就强迫他们去寻找答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, you've been part of hiring processes.
zh: 我是说，你参与过招聘流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every hiring process at a well-run company, it's somebody's job to make sure every question a candidate might ask, there's a frequently asked questions document with the answer.
zh: 在一家运营良好的公司里，每一次招聘流程中，确保回答候选人可能提出的每一个问题都是某人的工作，并且有一份包含答案的常见问题文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: So she's going to have to ask her boss, who's going to have to ask her boss and her boss.
zh: 所以她不得不去问她的老板，她的老板又不得不去问她老板的老板。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been in boardrooms where this kind of thing comes up, where someone's like, "We're getting this really irritating question from candidates and we don't know how to answer it."
zh: 我在董事会会议室里见过这种事情出现，有人说，“我们从候选人那里收到了这个非常烦人的问题，我们不知道如何回答。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, maybe just by asking the CEO also listens to Lenny, because everyone listens to Lenny.
zh: 现在，也许仅仅通过询问，CEO 也会听 Lenny 的，因为每个人都听 Lenny 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The CEO's also been thinking about doing it too, but he was like, "I don't know if I have the support of the board.
zh: CEO 也一直在考虑这样做，但他会想，“我不知道我是否有董事会的支持。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not really that courageous to bring it up."
zh: 我真的没有勇气提出来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you've created an excuse.
zh: 现在你创造了一个借口。

speaker: Unknown
en: A, we got to do it because I mean, it's the thing that everyone's doing it.
zh: A，我们必须这样做，因为我的意思是，这是每个人都在做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Employees are even starting to ask about it."
zh: 员工甚至开始询问这个了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If you read these postmortems of some of these crazy things that are going on in the AI wars, a surprising percentage of the motivation of leadership is like, we want to keep our employees happy.
zh: 如果你阅读一些关于 AI 战争中发生的这些疯狂事情的回顾，领导层很大一部分动机出人意料地是，我们想让员工保持快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: So be an unhappy employee who wants to know what's our mission.
zh: 所以做一个想知道我们的使命是什么的不快乐的员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it the real thing?
zh: 这是真的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So of course, that's not the only thing you can do, but that is the beginning, the easiest one on the integrity side.
zh: 所以当然，这不是你唯一能做的事情，但这是开始，是在诚信方面最容易做到的一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, a company that hasn't done it is very much at risk of eventually being betrayed.
zh: 同样，一家没有这样做的公司非常有可能最终被背叛。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are there any downsides to filing this charter?
zh: 提交这份章程有什么缺点吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the one thing that has no trade, truly no trade-offs at all.
zh: 这是唯一一件没有权衡，真正没有任何权衡的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, maybe you'll meet an investor who's suspicious about or doesn't like it.
zh: 我是说，也许你会遇到一位对此表示怀疑或不喜欢它的投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: But again, the only situation that would ever become relevant is if the investor is trying to force you to sell the company and you don't want to.
zh: 但同样，唯一会产生影响的情况是，如果投资者试图强迫你出售公司而你不愿意。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you could just tell them, "Are you telling me that in that situation you believe you should get to decide instead of me?"
zh: 所以你可以直接告诉他们，“你是在告诉我，在那种情况下，你相信应该由你而不是我来决定吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that what you're saying?
zh: 这就是你的意思吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you can ask yourself, "Is this really the right partner for you?"
zh: 然后你可以问自己，“这真的是适合我的合作伙伴吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone who would say yes to that, "What are we doing here?"
zh: 一个会对那个问题说是的人，“我们在这里干什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: All these feces are like, "I'm founder friendly.
zh: 所有这些废物都会说，“我对创始人友好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe in your vision."
zh: 我相信你的愿景。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you?
zh: 你是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you?
zh: 你是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or what's going on here?
zh: 还是这里发生了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's really important.
zh: 所以这真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other downside, the main downside of almost every technique in this book is that people will waste your time trying to talk you out of it.
zh: 另一个缺点，这本书中几乎每一个技术的主要缺点是，人们会浪费你的时间试图说服你放弃它。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to be ready for that.
zh: 你必须为此做好准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: The book actually has a whole section called how to talk to your investors and a whole separate section about how to talk to your lawyers, detailed exactly what to say, what are the questions you're likely to get because in the years I've been...
zh: 这本书实际上有一个整节叫做如何与你的投资者交谈，还有一个单独的整节关于如何与你的律师交谈，详细说明了该说什么，你可能会遇到什么问题，因为在过去的几年里我一直……

speaker: Unknown
en: I worked in the book for years.
zh: 我在这本书上工作了多年。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have been writing down all the objections I've heard from all the companies I've helped.
zh: 我一直记下我从我帮助过的所有公司那里听到的所有反对意见。

speaker: Unknown
en: I always tell me, if someone objects, please call me and tell me what they said.
zh: 我总是告诉我自己，如果有人反对，请打电话给我，告诉我说了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just wrote them all down.
zh: 我只是把它们都写下来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So all the answers are there.
zh: 所以所有的答案都在那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you talk to your lawyers, they'll say something like, "Well, you might want to just keep your options open.
zh: 但如果你和你的律师谈，他们会说类似这样的话，“嗯，你可能想保留你的选择余地。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't want to prematurely commit to your purpose."
zh: 你不想过早地承诺你的宗旨。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember if I hear that, I'm like, "Really?
zh: 我记得如果我听到那个，我会像，“真的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Keep my options open?
zh: 保留我的选择余地？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how you get maximum value?
zh: 那就是你获得最大价值的方式？

speaker: Unknown
en: What about the option to convert my customers into Soylent Green and eat them?
zh: 把我的客户转化成 Soylent Green 并吃掉他们的选项怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I have to keep that option open?"
zh: 我必须保留那个选项敞开吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You ask your lawyers this question, they will say something like, "You never know what you might need to do."
zh: 你问你的律师这个问题，他们会说类似这样的话，“你永远不知道你可能需要做什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we're like, "Why does nobody trust me?"
zh: 然后我们会像，“为什么没人信任我？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, because you won't even take that off the table.
zh: 嗯，因为你甚至不愿意把那个从桌上拿掉。

speaker: Unknown
en: Come on, man.
zh: 拜托，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, if this one has no downside, absolutely, absolute no-brainer.
zh: 不，如果这个没有下行风险，绝对，绝对是毫无疑问的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This sounds like a great single takeaway.
zh: 这听起来是一个很棒的单个要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: If there's anything you take away with this conversation, you should do this.
zh: 如果这次谈话中你有什么带走的东西，你应该做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that Anthropic did this is such a huge deal because I think a criticism of all of this that people probably hear is this is going to limit our growth, this is going to hurt our potential.
zh: Anthropic 这样做的事实是一件如此大事，因为我认为人们对这一切的一个批评可能是，这会限制我们的增长，这会损害我们的潜力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want to be this touchy-feely business.
zh: 我们想成为这种充满人情味的业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Capitalism has worked in creating a lot of very successful companies and value and innovation.
zh: 资本主义在创建许多非常成功的公司、价值和创新方面奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Capitalism has worked in many ways.
zh: 资本主义在许多方面都奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why would we need to do this?
zh: 为什么我们需要做这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: And Anthropic being the fastest growing company of all time, just like absurd, breaking all records is a Public Benefits Corporation should make you feel okay about doing this.
zh: 而 Anthropic 作为有史以来增长最快的公司，就像荒谬的一样，打破所有记录，是一家公益企业，这应该让你对做这个感觉没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, yeah.
zh: 噢，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anthropic has helped so much because they do many of the things in the book, including this, what they have.
zh: Anthropic 帮了这么多忙，因为他们做了书中的许多事情，包括这个，他们所拥有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can talk about the long-term benefit trust.
zh: 我们可以谈谈长期利益信托。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had that two-tiered governance structure that we were talking about before.
zh: 他们有我们之前谈论的那种双层治理结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's so funny, even now, even with Anthropic having such success, people still say stuff like this like, "Oh, I'm worried that I'm not going to be able to raise money."
zh: 但这很有趣，即使是现在，即使 Anthropic 取得了这样的成功，人们仍然说这样的话，像，“噢，我担心我无法筹集资金。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Anthropic's been able to raise some money."
zh: 我会像，“Anthropic 已经能够筹集一些钱了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That has not been an obstacle.
zh: 那并不是一个障碍。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, if you ask people, why is Anthropic winning?
zh: 事实上，如果你问人们，为什么 Anthropic 在赢？

speaker: Unknown
en: They will often cite some surface characteristic.
zh: 他们通常会引用一些表面特征。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anthropic very famously has lower inference costs than their competitors.
zh: Anthropic 非常出名地拥有比竞争对手更低的推理成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have faster product velocity than their competitors.
zh: 他们拥有比竞争对手更快的产品迭代速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do I hear?
zh: 我听到了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sometimes they say that they have more focus, better focus than their competitors.
zh: 有时他们说他们比竞争对手有更多专注，更好的专注。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are the most common ones I hear.
zh: 这些是我听到的最常见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for each of those, if you say, "Well, why?"
zh: 而对于每一个，如果你说，“嗯，为什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, they have lower inference costs.
zh: 嗯，他们有更低的推理成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have better technical infrastructure.
zh: 他们有更好的技术基础设施。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, because they had some kind of internal breakthrough.
zh: 嗯，因为他们有某种内部突破。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: If you keep asking why eventually something like, "Well, because they have the best talent."
zh: 如果你一直问为什么，最终会是类似这样的话，“嗯，因为他们有最好的人才。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because people want to work there.
zh: 因为人想去那里工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And you [inaudible 01:07:52] because people very much want to work.
zh: 然后你 [听不清 01:07:52] 因为人非常想工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want to work for the good guys.
zh: 他们想为好人工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: They think these people, they have the mission to save the world.
zh: 他们认为这些人，他们有拯救世界的使命。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why do they have that mission?
zh: 他们为什么会有那个使命？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because that was the ethos.
zh: 因为那是那个精神信条。

speaker: Unknown
en: But why have they been able to protect it?
zh: 但为什么他们能够保护它？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they paired the ethos with the integrity.
zh: 因为他们把那个精神信条与正直结合在了一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is at the heart of almost every breakthrough success story you've ever heard.
zh: 这几乎处于你听说过的每一个突破性成功故事的核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: You will find this lurking there as the thing that gave them the competitive advantage to drive that differentiation.
zh: 你会发现它潜伏在那里，作为给他们提供竞争优势来推动那种差异化的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny you say that.
zh: 你那样说很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I just had Kat, the head of product and Claude code, and I asked her just how are you able to ship so fast?
zh: 我刚才和 Kat，Claude code 的产品负责人在一起，我问她你们怎么能发布这么快？

speaker: Unknown
en: They're shipping a massive product or feature every week.
zh: 他们每周都在发布一个巨大的产品或功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Essentially, they were going through a period of force every day.
zh: 本质上，他们每天都在经历一个强制的时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a big part of our answer was exactly what you're describing, which is we are so mission aligned.
zh: 她回答的很大一部分正是你所描述的，也就是我们是如此使命一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so easy for us to decide this is something we will do or not.
zh: 对我们来说，决定做还是不做这件事很容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the thing.
zh: 那就是关键。

speaker: Unknown
en: Remember how we talked about how people say business is hard?
zh: 还记得我们怎么谈论人们说商业很难吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people have never worked in a mission-aligned company.
zh: 大多数人从未在一家使命一致的公司工作过。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is the organizational equivalent of being in the flow state as an individual person.
zh: 这作为一个处于心流状态的个人在组织层面的对应物。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a great feeling.
zh: 那是一种很棒的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it just makes everything easier.
zh: 它只是让一切变得更容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to have meetings about stuff.
zh: 你不必为事情开会。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have mission misalignment.
zh: 你没有使命不一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: In most organizations, you have people who I call the torchbearers, which are like the rare person in an organization who's simply committed to doing the right thing no matter what.
zh: 在大多数组织中，你有那些我称之为火炬手的人，他们就像组织中那个罕见的、无论如何都致力于做正确事情的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Steve Jobs very famously would have skip level meetings.
zh: Steve Jobs 非常有名地会举行越级会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: He wanted to meet not with his direct subordinates.
zh: 他想见的不是他的直接下属。

speaker: Unknown
en: He didn't care.
zh: 他不在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: He wanted to meet with the torchbearers throughout the organization.
zh: 他想见整个组织中的火炬手。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are the people that are going to drive us forward.
zh: 那些是会推动我们前进的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a torchbearer, you're just like, think about the designer who simply won't ship slop no matter what.
zh: 如果你是一个火炬手，你就只是，想想那个无论如何都不会发布垃圾的设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: The engineer who will not sacrifice quality or performance.
zh: 那个不会牺牲质量或性能的工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: You meet these people every once in a while, product manager who just...
zh: 你偶尔会遇到这些人，那个只是……的产品经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: They are prioritizing the right things, even if people complain.
zh: 他们正在优先考虑正确的事情，即使人们抱怨。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a traditional company, if you have that job, if people listening, if you've ever had this job, man, life sucks.
zh: 在一家传统公司中，如果你有那份工作，如果听众中有人，如果你曾经有过那份工作，伙计，生活糟透了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every fricking day, someone's in your office with a spreadsheet being like, "But what is the ROI of doing the right thing?"
zh: 该死的每一天，都有人拿着电子表格进你的办公室说，“但是做正确事情的 ROI 是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're just like, "I don't know.
zh: 而你只是像，“我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just the right thing."
zh: 那只是正确的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: In a mission-aligned company, this doesn't happen two different ways.
zh: 在一家使命一致的公司，这不会以两种不同的方式发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's two different antibodies that protect against this.
zh: 有两种不同的抗体可以防止这种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, since everyone's mission aligned, no one's bothering to make the spreadsheet.
zh: 首先，既然大家的使命一致，就没有人费心去制作电子表格。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no need.
zh: 没有必要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The late great Clayton Christensen once said that it's easier to do the right thing 100% of the time than 98% of the time.
zh: 已故的伟大的 Clayton Christensen 曾经说过，100% 的时间做正确的事情比 98% 的时间做更容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like because now you don't have to have a meeting about it.
zh: 就像因为现在你不必为此开会。

speaker: Unknown
en: At Anthropic people, someone's like, "Oh, I came up with this crazy unsafe thing that will make us an extra dollar."
zh: 在 Anthropic 人们，有人说，“哦，我想出了这个疯狂的、不安全的东西，可以让我们多赚一美元。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't have to have a meeting about it.
zh: 我们不必为此开会。

speaker: Unknown
en: I already tell you we're not going to do it.
zh: 我已经告诉你我们不会去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: We already know.
zh: 我们已经知道了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So everyone's incredibly aligned.
zh: 所以大家都非常一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the second thing that I think is less appreciated is a practice I call the culture bank, which is when you start to see trustworthiness as an asset, you start to realize that certain actions build that asset and others take it away.
zh: 但我认为第二件被低估的事情是我称之为文化银行的实践，也就是当你开始把可信赖性视为一种资产时，你开始意识到某些行动会建立那种资产，而其他行动会消耗它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me teach you a rule I learned from one of my favorite founders, Todd Park.
zh: 所以让我教给你一条我从我最喜欢的创始人之一 Todd Park 那里学到的规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: He created Devoted Health, the health insurance company I was mentioning before.
zh: 他创建了 Devoted Health，我之前提到的那家健康保险公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: But he learned it from Howard Schultz who built Starbucks.
zh: 但他是从建立了星巴克的 Howard Schultz 那里学到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you see in strong culture companies, you always see this pattern where you've taught people that whenever you do the right thing, that is you do something in defense of the company's values that has a sacrifice to it.
zh: 如果你在强大的文化公司中看，你总是看到这种模式，你教给了人们每当你做正确的事情，也就是你做了某些维护公司价值观但有牺牲的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Saying the values, enchanting them, making a song out of them, that's not it.
zh: 说出那些价值观，去美化它们，把它们编成歌，那不是那回事。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you do something, I tell a story about this grocery store in Texas called H-E-B, where the power went out, there was an ice storm, and the manager let all the customers just take their groceries home.
zh: 但如果你做了点什么，我讲一个关于德克萨斯州一家叫 H-E-B 的杂货店的故事，那里停电了，有一场冰暴，经理让所有顾客直接把杂货带回家。

speaker: Unknown
en: No charge, because the point of sale system wasn't working.
zh: 不收钱，因为销售点系统不工作了。

speaker: Unknown
en: People say, "Oh, what a courageous manager."
zh: 人们说，“噢，多么勇敢的经理。”

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what they train people in at H-E-B, that you're making a deposit in the culture bank when you do the right thing.
zh: 那是他们在 H-E-B 培训员工的内容，当你做正确的事情时，你是在文化银行里存入一笔存款。

speaker: Unknown
en: So deposits, you make a sacrifice.
zh: 所以存款，就是你做出牺牲。

speaker: Unknown
en: A withdrawal is the opposite.
zh: 取款是相反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You do something greedy, self-interested for the organization.
zh: 你为了组织做了贪婪的、利己的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: The Todd Park rule, as I call it, everyone who hears this rule, the first time they hear it tells me it's impossible and you can't do it.
zh: 我称之为 Todd Park 规则，每个听到这个规则的人，第一次听到时都告诉我这是不可能的，你做不到。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't make sense, but this is the rule, is really simple.
zh: 这听起来不理智，但这条规则其实非常简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: Only make deposits, never make withdrawals.
zh: 只做存款，绝不做取款。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's it.
zh: 就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you're going to make withdrawals by accident sometimes because you can make mistakes, but you never intentionally make a withdrawal.
zh: 因为你有时会意外地取款，因为你可能会犯错，但你永远不要有意地取款。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that I think is the true power.
zh: 我认为这才是真正的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you hear someone who's living that flow, like Kat, you were talking about in Anthropic, that has been internalized in the organization.
zh: 当你听到一个活在这种流动中的人，就像你刚才谈到 Anthropic 的 Kat 那样，那已经在组织内化了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's what the pioneering management theorist, Mary Parker Follett called the invisible leader.
zh: 这就是那位先驱管理理论家 Mary Parker Follett 所说的无形领袖。

speaker: Unknown
en: The person, the thing that people follow even when no manager is present.
zh: 那个人，那个即使没有管理者在场人们也会跟随的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So no one has to be in the room to remind you to do it.
zh: 所以不需要有人在房间里提醒你去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just do it because you've internalized that this is what we're all about.
zh: 你就去做，因为你已经内化了这就是我们的全部意义。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when everyone around you is doing that same thing, the whole velocity of the whole organization increases exponentially.
zh: 当你周围的所有人都在做同样的事情时，整个组织的整个速度呈指数级增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to hear the OpenAI versus Anthropic story because you've mentioned a part of the structural pieces of this nonprofit.
zh: 我想听听 OpenAI 对比 Anthropic 的故事，因为你提到了这个非营利组织的结构性部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So talk about just what happened there and how that relates to what you talk about versus Anthropic.
zh: 所以谈谈那里发生了什么，以及这与你谈论的关于 Anthropic 的内容有何关联。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I actually want to hear a little bit about just the actual structure.
zh: 然后我其实还想稍微了解一下实际的结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is this two-tier thing?
zh: 这个双层结构是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the nonprofit piece?
zh: 非营利部分是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: OpenAI is a really hard case study to learn from because it's such a bizarre story and it involves mega personalities like Elon and Sam dueling to the death, so it's complicated.
zh: OpenAI 是一个很难从中学习的案例研究，因为这是一个如此离奇的故事，而且涉及像 Elon 和 Sam 这样的大人物生死对决，所以很复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we can get into it if you want.
zh: 但如果你想，我们可以深入聊聊。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I entered the story.
zh: 但我进入了这个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The funny part is like, I played a very bit role in all this.
zh: 有趣的是，我在其中扮演了一个非常次要的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm not an important actor in this story at all.
zh: 所以我在这个故事里根本不是什么重要的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: I take no credit for Anthropic success, all the credit to Dario and Daniella and the whole team.
zh: 我不居功于 Anthropic 的成功，所有功劳归于 Dario 和 Daniella 以及整个团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when they left OpenAI, this is two or three OpenAI crises ago, depending on how you count.
zh: 但当他们离开 OpenAI 时，这是两三轮 OpenAI 危机之前的事了，取决于你怎么算。

speaker: Unknown
en: They left and they wanted to start Anthropic.
zh: 他们离开了，他们想创办 Anthropic。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now today, people are like, "Oh, sure it works for Anthropic.
zh: 如今，人们会说，“噢，当然这对 Anthropic 适用。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're a world beating company."
zh: 他们是一家世界级的公司。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But Dario was a first time founder.
zh: 但 Dario 是一个首次创业者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was there.
zh: 我当时在那。

speaker: Unknown
en: He was impressive, but in the way that a technical founder for the first time is impressive.
zh: 他令人印象深刻，但那种技术型首次创业者令人印象深刻的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he had investors who were excited to back him, but they were like effective altruism, like true believers.
zh: 他有投资者很兴奋支持他，但他们是那种有效利他主义，真正的信徒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't the top venture fund.
zh: 不是顶级风投基金。

speaker: Unknown
en: None of the top venture funds wanted to participate in this round.
zh: 顶级风投基金都没有参与这一轮。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't a hot company at all by modern standards.
zh: 按现代标准看，这根本不是一家热门公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And generative AI, the boom hadn't happened yet.
zh: 而且生成式 AI 的繁荣还没发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: ChatGPT hadn't been invented yet.
zh: ChatGPT 还没发明出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the mass psychosis we're all living through had not occurred yet.
zh: 我们都在经历的这种群体性精神错乱还没发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nonetheless, they were true believers in this safety mission and so one of their investors suggested they come talk to me.
zh: 尽管如此，他们是这个安全使命的真正信徒，所以他们的一位投资者建议他们来找我聊聊。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone knew I was like a eccentric collector of weird ideas.
zh: 大家都知道我就像个怪异想法的古怪收藏家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some people collect butterfly wings or whatever.
zh: 有些人收集蝴蝶翅膀什么的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I collected alternative governance ideas.
zh: 我收集另类治理想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when they came to me, I was walking them through the same horror story I'm telling you today.
zh: 所以当他们来找我时，我正在给他们讲我今天告诉你的同样的恐怖故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I told them, "Look, if you don't get this right, here's what's going to happen."
zh: 我告诉他们，“听着，如果你们不把这事儿做对，接下来会发生这样的事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they were very determined to do something about it.
zh: 而且他们非常下定决心要对此做点什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we talked about what we should do.
zh: 于是我们讨论了应该做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I advised, again, a very little bit.
zh: 而我再次给出了很少的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't take the credit for what happened next, but they wrote into their charter that they were going to do this.
zh: 我不对接下来发生的事邀功，但他们在章程中写明他们打算这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were a PBC from the very beginning and they wrote into their charter that they had the right to enact these additional reforms, which they had to defend, all credit to them for taking it seriously, for convincing their investors.
zh: 他们从一开始就是一个 PBC（公益公司），并在章程中写入他们有权实施这些额外的改革，他们必须为此辩护，所有归功于他们认真对待此事，说服了他们的投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they had to defend it for two years because they didn't actually implement what's called now the long-term benefit trust until their series C, but they had the right and the intention to do it in all their legal documents from inception.
zh: 而且他们必须为此辩护两年，因为他们直到 C 轮融资才真正实施所谓的长期利益信托，但他们从一开始就在所有法律文件中拥有这样做的权利和意图。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was a really important choice.
zh: 那真是一个非常重要的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so even today, Anthropic has directors on its for profit board who are appointed by and are accountable to an outside group of trustees who are AI safety experts who do not have equity in Anthropic.
zh: 所以即使在今天，Anthropic 在其盈利董事会有董事，他们是由一个外部受托人小组任命并对其负责的，这些人是 AI 安全专家，他们在 Anthropic 没有股权。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they do not have a fund-
zh: 所以他们没有资-

speaker: Unknown
en: The experts who do not have equity in Anthropic.
zh: 那些在 Anthropic 没有股权的专家。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they do not have a financial incentive.
zh: 所以他们没有经济激励。

speaker: Unknown
en: In its growth, they have an incentive to see it done properly.
zh: 在其发展中，他们有动力看到它被正确地完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: So whenever you see Anthropic, do the right thing.
zh: 所以每当你看到 Anthropic 做正确的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like when they refuse to release a model because they think it's too dangerous, think about how much that's costing them.
zh: 就像当他们拒绝发布一个模型，因为他们认为它太危险时，想想那要花费他们多少代价。

speaker: Unknown
en: People say, "Well, they do it for the publicity," but publicity's nice, but you know what would be really nice?
zh: 人们说，“嗯，他们这样做是为了宣传，”但宣传固然好，但你知道什么真的好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is having the number one top model that everyone has to pay you to use.
zh: 是拥有每个人都必须付费使用的头号模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's really nice.
zh: 那真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: People say that they got into this fight with the Pentagon.
zh: 人们说他们卷入了与五角大楼的争斗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to be clear, I don't think that they're even the primary actor in that story.
zh: 明确地说，我不认为他们甚至不是那个故事里的主要行动者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a story of government overreached and they were an impossible situation.
zh: 这是一个政府越权的故事，他们处于一种不可能的境地。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even people who say they did the wrong things, when you're an impossible situation, what are you going to do?
zh: 即使那些说他们做错事的人，当你处于不可能的境地时，你能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the Kobayashi Maru.
zh: 这是一个 Kobayashi Maru（小林丸）。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you going to do?
zh: 你能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're in an impossible situation.
zh: 你处于一种不可能的境地。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no right answer.
zh: 没有正确的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: But even people who think they did the wrong thing, tactically speaking, admire them because we live in a time when it's so rare for companies to turn down money ever.
zh: 但即使是那些认为他们做错了事的人，从战术角度来说，也佩服他们，因为在我们这个时代，公司拒绝资金是如此罕见。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here they turn down... Say what you want about them, but they turned down a $200 million contract and bore the wrath of the world's largest army and government.
zh: 而在这里他们拒绝了……随你怎么说，但他们拒绝了一份 2 亿美元的合同，并承受了世界上最大的军队和政府的愤怒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: That took a lot of courage.
zh: 那需要很大的勇气。

speaker: Unknown
en: Part of that courage is enabled by the fact that they have this structure and investors can't just oust Dario on a moment's notice.
zh: 这种勇气的部分原因是他们拥有这种结构，投资者不能立刻罢免 Dario。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the structure, frankly, is better than founder control.
zh: 我认为坦率地说，这种结构比创始人控制更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dario does not have dual class shares the way that Mark Zuckerberg or Larry and Sergey had.
zh: Dario 没有像 Mark Zuckerberg 或 Larry 和 Sergey 那样的双重股权结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more institutional in its nature.
zh: 它在本质上更具制度性。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to kind of answer your question, the critical thing we need, if we're going to really resist outside pressure, we need what I call a mission guardian.
zh: 但为了回答你的问题，如果我们真的要抵制外部压力，我们需要的关键东西，就是我所谓的任务守护者。

speaker: Unknown
en: It has to be somebody or some entity's job to make sure that the thing remains mission locked or mission aligned.
zh: 这必须是某人或某个实体的工作，以确保该事物保持任务锁定或任务一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: That does not happen by accident because gravity is such a powerful force.
zh: 这不会偶然发生，因为重力是一种如此强大的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Anthropic's solution to that is to have a mission guardian in the form of the long-term benefit trust.
zh: 所以 Anthropic 对此的解决方案是以长期利益信托的形式拥有一个任务守护者。

speaker: Unknown
en: OpenAI very famously had the nonprofit foundation, although now they've converted to a public benefit corp structure.
zh: OpenAI 著名地拥有非营利基金会，尽管现在他们已转换为公益公司结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously Google and Facebook are protected by founder control.
zh: 显然 Google 和 Facebook 受到创始人控制保护。

speaker: Unknown
en: The founder is the mission guardian.
zh: 创始人就是任务守护者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are the structures.
zh: 这些就是那些结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I tell the story in the book, a very weird experience I had.
zh: 我在书中讲了这个故事，我有过的一个非常奇怪的经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was literally at the Vatican of all places.
zh: 我真的在梵蒂冈，在所有地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, look at me.
zh: 我的意思是，看看我。

speaker: Unknown
en: What am I doing at the Vatican?
zh: 我在梵蒂冈做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was cool.
zh: 但那很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: [inaudible 01:17:06] had convened a conference on AI governance and they invited me to speak.
zh: ……召集了一场关于 AI 治理的会议，他们邀请我去发言。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I was on this panel at the Vatican with every other AI company and me.
zh: 所以我当时在梵蒂冈的一个小组讨论上，还有其他所有的 AI 公司和我。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was weird.
zh: 很奇怪。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was actually strange.
zh: 确实很怪。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like me, literally me.
zh: 就像我，真的是我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I go down.
zh: 我走下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was looking down the row.
zh: 我看着这一排人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Me, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cohere, Palantir, everyone on this panel together.
zh: 我，Anthropic，OpenAI，Google，Cohere，Palantir，所有人都在这个小组讨论里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I looked on the panel and I realized not a single one of these companies has standard governance.
zh: 我看着那个小组讨论，我意识到这些公司中没有一家拥有标准的治理结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how bad it is.
zh: 糟糕到这种程度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody making this technology would say, "Oh yes, standard governance, that's fine.
zh: 没有制造这项技术的人会说，“噢是的，标准治理，那没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure that..." It's just too dangerous.
zh: 我确信……”它太危险了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they of course take different approaches to mission guardianship, some of which are better and some are worse.
zh: 但他们当然对使命守护采取了不同的方法，其中一些更好，一些更差。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like founder control I think actually has a lot of downsides to it.
zh: 就像创始人控制我认为实际上有很多弊端。

speaker: Unknown
en: So and when you say standard governance, every one of these AI companies is not doing it the way every other startup was doing?
zh: 所以当你说标准治理时，这些 AI 公司中没有一家是按照其他每家初创公司那样做的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: They understood they needed to do something different to protect-
zh: 他们明白他们需要做点不同的事情来保护——

speaker: Eric Ries
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... humanity essentially from AI?
zh: ……本质上是人类免受 AI 的伤害？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，正是。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because otherwise it's just this technology is so valuable.
zh: 因为否则这项技术太有价值了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you say that shareholders should run it, then you're saying literally whoever can borrow the most money should be able to control this technology.
zh: 如果你说股东应该运营它，那么你实际上是在说，谁能借到最多的钱，谁就能控制这项技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just, that's nuts.
zh: 这简直，太荒谬了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That can't be how it works.
zh: 不能是这样的运作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: No way.
zh: 没门。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think any company should be governed that way, but certainly not AI companies.
zh: 我认为没有任何公司应该那样治理，但绝对不是 AI 公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Certainly not.
zh: 绝对不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 因为你就像 Atlas，你甚至不能耸肩。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just to kind of plant these things in people's heads as they, okay, we need to really think about this stuff.
zh: 所以只是为了让人们脑子里有这些概念，好的，我们需要真正思考这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a nonprofit approach to this, there's... What are the terms that people should just think about?
zh: 有一种非营利的方法，还有……人们应该思考的术语是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So we talked about we need a mission guardian.
zh: 所以我们谈到我们需要一个使命守护者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So first question, is the mission guardian a person or a thing?
zh: 所以第一个问题是，使命守护者是一个人还是一个事物？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's our kind of first decision point.
zh: 这是我们的第一个决策点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, some, I think for early stage companies, founder control is fine as a good bridge, a temporary bridge to a more permanent structure.
zh: 所以是的，有些，我认为对于早期阶段的公司，创始人控制作为一个很好的桥梁，通往更永久结构的临时桥梁，是可以的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And some companies get really far with founder control.
zh: 有些公司凭借创始人控制走得很远。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's okay.
zh: 那没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: But a lot of founders who have founder control wind up really miserable, as you can see by the fact they're having a mental health breakdown right in front of us, all of us on social media basically every day.
zh: 但是很多拥有创始人控制的创始人最终非常痛苦，正如你可以看到的事实，他们就在我们面前精神崩溃，基本上我们每天在社交媒体上都能看到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you become like Atlas, you can't even shrug.
zh: 因为你变得像 Atlas 一样，你甚至无法耸肩。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's you holding back the abyss.
zh: 是你在抵挡深渊。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a lot.
zh: 很沉重。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think a better, more permanent solution is to encode the protection into the structure.
zh: 所以我认为一个更好的、更永久的解决方案是将保护写入结构中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now that can be done with a single entity like Costco.
zh: 现在这可以通过单一实体来完成，比如 Costco。

speaker: Unknown
en: Costco just has the rules written right into the structure itself.
zh: Costco 只是把这些规则直接写进结构本身。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that means that every time they lose one of the structures, every once in a while when an attack chips off a little bit of it, they don't have any way to grow them back.
zh: 但这意味着每当他们失去其中一个结构，偶尔当攻击敲掉其中的一小部分时，他们没有任何办法让它重新生长。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's like you've built this fortress, but you don't have a way to renew it.
zh: 就像你建造了这个堡垒，但你没有办法更新它。

speaker: Unknown
en: The better way, I think, according to the evidence, is to have some stakeholder, somebody be the steward of the mission and have a way to renew that person, that set of people.
zh: 我认为根据证据，更好的办法是有某个利益相关者，某个人成为使命的管家，并且有一种办法更新那个人，那一群人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So some people accomplish that by what's called an employee ownership trust.
zh: 所以有些人通过所谓的员工持股信托来实现这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the employees are the mission guardians, like the John Lewis Partnership in the UK is a famous example.
zh: 所以员工是使命守护者，就像英国的 John Lewis Partnership 是一个著名的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously cooperatives have this...
zh: 显然合作社有这个……

speaker: Unknown
en: This can work in a cooperative at scale.
zh: 这可以在大规模合作中运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mondragon, for example, in Spain has like 80,000 employees.
zh: 例如，西班牙的 Mondragon 有大约 80,000 名员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a huge company, but they're all employee cooperatives.
zh: 这是一家巨大的公司，但它们都是员工合作社。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can do it through an employee voting trust.
zh: 你可以通过员工投票信托来实现这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how Alibaba is protected, where the employees vote for the board members rather than the reverse.
zh: 这就是阿里巴巴受到保护的方式，员工投票选举董事会成员，而不是反过来。

speaker: Unknown
en: But those are much more complicated compared to me, the two simplest solutions are either a nonprofit foundation as in the Novo Nordisk example, or in the case of what's called the Perpetual Purpose Trust or PPT.
zh: 但那些对我来说要复杂得多，两个最简单的解决方案要么是像 Novo Nordisk 案例那样的非营利基金会，要么是在所谓的 Perpetual Purpose Trust 或 PPT 的情况下。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Perpetual Purpose Trust is a non-economic entity.
zh: 而且 Perpetual Purpose Trust 是一个非经济实体。

speaker: Unknown
en: So whereas the Novo Nordisk Foundation is the largest charitable foundation in the world because it owns a big chunk of Novo Nordisk and that's worked out pretty well.
zh: 因此，鉴于 Novo Nordisk 基金会是世界上最大的慈善基金会，因为它拥有 Novo Nordisk 的大部分股份，而且运作得相当好。

speaker: Unknown
en: The Anthropic long-term benefit trust has no economic dimension to it at all.
zh: Anthropic 的长期利益信托根本没有经济维度。

speaker: Unknown
en: It only has the mission, oversight, responsibility.
zh: 它只有使命、监督和责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what's nice about a Perpetual Purpose Trust, as in the case of Patagonia is governed with a purpose trust also, is you have the trustees, but then you also have someone whose job is actually to sue the trustees if they ever deviate from the mission.
zh: 而 Perpetual Purpose Trust 的好处，就像 Patagonia 的情况一样也是由目的信托治理的，是你有受托人，然后你还有一个人，他的工作实际上是在受托人偏离使命时起诉他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you actually have what's called the purpose protector who's an extra person who can get in there and say, "If things go wrong."
zh: 所以你实际上有一个所谓的 purpose protector（目的保护人），他是一个额外的人，可以介入并说，“如果出了问题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, it's like you have checks and balances like in a government.
zh: 所以对我来说，这就像你在政府中拥有的制衡机制。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more stable.
zh: 它更稳定。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a more stable structure.
zh: 这是一个更稳定的结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: To me though, I use the omnibus term spiritual holding company to describe this category of things.
zh: 不过对我来说，我使用统称精神控股公司来描述这一类事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I said before, we have a lot of infighting.
zh: 因为正如我之前所说，我们有很多内斗。

speaker: Unknown
en: The people who advocate for each of these things thinks their version is the best.
zh: 那些倡导每一种方案的人认为他们的版本是最好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't even mention the Evergreen, the Tugboat Foundation.
zh: 我甚至没有提到 Evergreen，那个 Tugboat Foundation。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't believe in having investors at all.
zh: 他们根本不相信有投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's people who think the solution to this is no investors involved, that makes the problem a lot easier.
zh: 所以有些人认为解决这个问题的办法是不涉及投资者，这让问题变得容易得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyway, there's a lot of different ways this can be done.
zh: 无论如何，有很多不同的方法可以做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think I even mentioned ESOPs.
zh: 我想我甚至没有提到 ESOPs。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just so many.
zh: 实在太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we need to have an omnibus term.
zh: 所以我们需要一个统称。

speaker: Unknown
en: I call it the spiritual holding company.
zh: 我称之为精神控股公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: The holding company, like the Berkshire Hathaway, but rather than having everything be wholly owned, it's the holding company for the spirit, the animating essence of the whole.
zh: 控股公司，就像 Berkshire Hathaway，但与其让所有东西都完全拥有，它是整体精神、驱动本质的控股公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's a lot of evidence in the book, a lot that this structure is more stable, more durable, more likely to invest in quality and R&D and the things we really care about and better for shareholders than the conventional structure.
zh: 书中有大量证据，这种结构更稳定、更持久、更有可能投资于质量、研发和我们真正关心的事情，而且对股东来说比传统结构更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: If this all sounds like a huge drag and a lot of work and really annoying, I'd say go back an hour when we talked a lot about just what is it you were trying to avoid and the pain.
zh: 如果这一切听起来像是一个巨大的负担，很多工作，真的很烦人，我想说回到一个小时前，我们谈论了很多关于你试图避免什么和痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the thing.
zh: 就是那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, you know what's really a drag?
zh: 就像，你知道什么是真正的负担吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll tell you a story.
zh: 我给你讲个故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: A friend of mine got ousted by his investors.
zh: 我的一个朋友被他的投资者赶走了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was going to this party to celebrate him.
zh: 我要去参加一个派对来庆祝他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was like people had flown in from all over the country to celebrate, including employees he laid off came to this party.
zh: 就像人们从全国各地飞来庆祝，包括他解雇的员工都来到了这个派对。

speaker: Unknown
en: It must have been a thousand people there.
zh: 那里一定有一千人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was describing to a new founder like, "I'm sorry, I can't help you with your company right now.
zh: 我正在向一位新创始人描述，“抱歉，我现在帮不了你的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got to go to this party."
zh: 我得去参加这个派对。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was like, "Okay."
zh: 他就像，“好的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm describing to him and he was just like, "Wow, what respect.
zh: 我正在向他描述，他就像，“哇，多受尊重啊。

speaker: Unknown
en: That founder sounds invested.
zh: 那位创始人听起来很投入。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just the kind of company I want to create."
zh: 就是我想创造的那种公司。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Dude, you have not been listening to anything I'm saying.
zh: 我就像，“兄弟，你没听我说的任何话。

speaker: Unknown
en: He doesn't work there anymore."
zh: 他不再在那里工作了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Sol Price.
zh: 他就像，“Sol Price。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not a party.
zh: 这不是一个派对。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is awake."
zh: 这是一个清醒。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was like, "What?
zh: 他就像，“什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Did he die?"
zh: 他死了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "No, man, he didn't die."
zh: “不，伙计，他没死。”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Did the company go bankrupt?"
zh: “公司破产了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "No, the company's fine.
zh: “不，公司很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not the problem.
zh: 那不是问题所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: The new..." And he was like, "Is the new CEO an asshole or something?"
zh: 新……”他就像，“新 CEO 是个混蛋之类的吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "No, I like the new CEO.
zh: 我就像，“不，我喜欢这位新 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's a friend of mine also.
zh: 他也是我的朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: Perfectly fine.
zh: 完全没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The problem is..." He's like, "What's the problem?"
zh: 问题在于……”他就像，“问题是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "The problem is if a company can be decapitated at any time, you can no longer trust it.
zh: “问题在于，如果一家公司随时可能被斩首，你就再也无法信任它了。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the promises that this company had made over its 15-year life, nobody believes them anymore."
zh: 这家公司在其 15 年生命周期中做出的所有承诺，现在再也没人相信了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The new CEO is going out and making new promises, but we're like, "An activist investor owning 0.5% of the company can oust you at any time.
zh: 新 CEO 正在四处做出新的承诺，但我们就像，“一个只拥有公司 0.5% 股份的激进投资者随时可以把你赶下台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why should I believe anything you say?"
zh: 我为什么要相信你说的任何话？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we're like, "Why is trust collapsing in our institutions across the board?"
zh: 然后我们就像，“为什么在我们的各类机构中，信任正在全面崩塌？”

speaker: Unknown
en: We are teaching a leadership philosophy that is antitrust, anti-trustworthy.
zh: 我们正在传授一种反信任、不值得信任的领导哲学。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, if it sounds like this sounds like a bummer to have to worry about this stuff, you know what's really a bummer?
zh: 所以是的，如果听起来必须担心这些事情很令人沮丧，你知道什么才真正令人沮丧吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Come to that party with me.
zh: 跟我来参加那个聚会。

speaker: Unknown
en: That sucks.

zh: 有一个视频，研究人员创造了他们称之为钢琴搬运工谜题的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You remember that famous clip?
zh: 你记得那个著名的片段吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you have the meme of Friends where they're trying to get the couch down the stairwell and he's like, "Pivot, pivot, pivot."
zh: 我不知道你是否有《老友记》那个表情包，他们试图把沙发搬下楼梯，他就像“Pivot，pivot，pivot”。

speaker: Unknown
en: People send it to me all the time for obvious reasons.
zh: 人们因为显而易见的原因一直发给我。

speaker: Unknown
en: They created a version of that puzzle that they had ants solve.
zh: 他们制作了一个让蚂蚁解决那个谜题的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: So visualizes like two slits, two walls with a gap in each wall and a big eye beam shaped irregular object.
zh: 所以视觉化像两条缝隙，两堵墙，每堵墙上有一个缺口，和一个大眼梁形状的不规则物体。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you watch a human solve the puzzle, it goes like this.
zh: 如果你观察人类解决这个谜题，它是这样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They try one thing, they think about it, they rearrange, you go back, you back.
zh: 他们尝试一件事，思考一下，重新安排，你回去，你后退。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've ever got to do a puzzle like that?
zh: 你做过这样的谜题吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You can just tell watching the video that an intelligent person is trying to solve this puzzle because they try logical things that don't work and then they learn.
zh: 你可以通过看视频告诉你，一个聪明的人正在试图解决这个谜题，因为他们尝试行不通的逻辑的事情，然后他们学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you give one ant this puzzle, he cannot solve it, obviously, but put a thousand ants in there and they can solve the puzzle.
zh: 如果你给一只蚂蚁这个谜题，它显然无法解决，但放一千只蚂蚁进去，它们就能解决谜题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you watch the video of the ant colony solving this puzzle, you will swear you can see an intelligence at work because it does just like a human.
zh: 如果你观察蚁群解决这个谜题的视频，你会发誓你能看到一种智能在工作，因为它就像人类一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It tries something, it pauses to consider, it reorients, tries something different, it's spooky.
zh: 它尝试一些事情，停下来考虑，重新定位，尝试不同的东西，这很怪诞。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the researchers found this is what we have to understand for humans.
zh: 研究人员发现这是我们必须为人类理解的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: The more ants you put in the puzzle, the faster the solution.
zh: 你在谜题中放入的蚂蚁越多，解决速度越快。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the more humans you add, the worse, unless the humans are very carefully aligned.
zh: 但是你增加的人类越多，情况越糟，除非这些人类非常仔细地对齐。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the key lesson for organizational design.
zh: 这是组织设计的关键教训。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are birthing these things left and right.
zh: 我们正在到处孕育这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if we don't tend to them properly, they develop emergent characteristics that we don't like.
zh: 如果我们不恰当地照料它们，它们会发展出我们不喜欢的涌现特征。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want that to be like that.
zh: 我们不希望那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And no amount of founder mode is going to clean that up because you're talking about something that is deep in the DNA of the thing you made.
zh: 任何数量的创始人模式都无法清理它，因为你说的是深藏在你所制造事物的 DNA 里的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Beware.
zh: 当心。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'm hearing here is we should be hiring more ants for our organizations.
zh: 我在这里听到的是我们应该为我们的组织雇佣更多的蚂蚁。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well...

speaker: Unknown
en: Eric, we've given people a lot to think about.
zh: Eric，我们已经给了人们很多值得思考的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's a lot of just like, "Oh wow, I should really think about this and take this seriously."
zh: 我想有很多就像，“噢哇，我真的应该思考一下这个并认真对待它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a final thought, final nugget, final lesson you want to leave listeners with before we get out of here?
zh: 最后的想法、最后的金块、最后你想留给听众的教训是什么，在我们离开这里之前？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about Mary Parker Follett.
zh: 让我们谈谈 Mary Parker Follett。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mentioned her in passing and I feel like, "You know what?
zh: 我顺便提到了她，我觉得，“你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet a bunch of people have never heard of her."
zh: 我打赌很多人从未听说过她。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me give you one more blast from the past.
zh: 所以让我再给你一个来自过去的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people have heard of someone named Frederick Winslow Taylor.
zh: 大多数人听说过一个叫 Frederick Winslow Taylor 的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fred Taylor's good friend of mine with a pioneering original management theorist.
zh: Fred Taylor 是我的好朋友，一位先锋的原创管理理论家。

speaker: Unknown
en: He wrote The Principles of Scientific Management in 1911, and Taylorism was one of the most popular management fads of all time.
zh: 他在 1911 年写了《科学管理原理》，泰勒主义是有史以来最流行的管理狂热之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think we have fads now, you should see Taylorism.
zh: 如果你认为我们现在有流行风潮，你应该看看泰勒主义。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like debated at the Supreme Court.
zh: 它就像是在最高法院辩论过一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was front page news in the 19 teens.
zh: 它在 1910 年代是头版新闻。

speaker: Unknown
en: Taylor, they made movies about him.
zh: 泰勒，他们为他拍了电影。

speaker: Unknown
en: He was an incredibly famous person.
zh: 他是一个非常有名的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But one of his contemporaries was a woman.
zh: 但是他的同时代人中有一位女性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Her name was Mary Parker Follett.
zh: 她的名字是 Mary Parker Follett。

speaker: Unknown
en: And her work is so far ahead of its time that if you read it today, you would think this person lived in 2026.
zh: 她的作品远远超前于它的时代，如果你今天读它，你会以为这个人生活在 2026 年。

speaker: Unknown
en: She would write things like, "We need to focus on power with, not power over."
zh: 她会写像这样的话，“我们需要关注权力与共，而不是权力凌驾。”

speaker: Unknown
en: She said, "The superior and the subordinate together obey the law of the situation," meaning we work together to figure out what the situation requires.
zh: 她说，“上级和下属一起服从情境法则，”意思是我们一起弄清楚情境需要什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't just tell the subordinate what to do.
zh: 我们不只是告诉下属做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: She said, "The job of a leader, the hallmark of a leader is, can they create more leaders?"
zh: 她说，“领导者的工作，领导者的标志是，他们能不能创造更多的领导者？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So if someone said that to you right now, you'd be like, "Oh, that's going on TikTok right this second."
zh: 所以如果有人现在对你说这话，你会说，“噢，这现在正在 TikTok 上发生。”

speaker: Lenny
en: Twitter.
zh: Twitter。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, "That's awesome.
zh: 就像，“那太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What podcast was that on?"
zh: 那是在哪个播客上说的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: No, she wrote that in 1920.
zh: 不，她在 1920 年就写了那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, unfortunately, for reasons you probably can guess, she was utterly erased from history.
zh: 现在，不幸的是，原因你可能猜得到，她完全从历史上被抹去了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Her work was utterly lost.
zh: 她的作品完全丢失了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody studied it at all for most of the 20th century.
zh: 20 世纪的大部分时间根本没有人研究它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it was totally rediscovered and it was republished in the 1990s.
zh: 然后它被完全重新发现，并在 1990 年代重新出版。

speaker: Unknown
en: The great Peter Drucker called her the "Prophet of management."
zh: 伟大的彼得·德鲁克称她为“管理的先知”。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one of her most important concepts is what she called the invisible leader.
zh: 所以她最重要的概念之一是她所谓的隐形领导者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just love this.
zh: 我就是喜欢这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just imagine someone, a woman in 1920 going around saying this to people, how it would blow their minds.
zh: 想象一下，一个 1920 年的女性到处跟人们说这个，这会如何颠覆他们的认知。

speaker: Unknown
en: She would say, "Mr. Rowntree, the owner of the Rowntree Chocolate Factory is not the leader of the Rowntree Chocolate Factory."
zh: 她会说，“Rowntree 先生，Rowntree 巧克力工厂的所有者，不是 Rowntree 巧克力工厂的领导者。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And people would be like, "Lady, what are you talking about?
zh: 人们会说，“女士，你在说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: His name is on the door.
zh: 他的名字就在门上。

speaker: Unknown
en: His family has owned this thing.
zh: 他的家族拥有这个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How?
zh: 怎么回事？

speaker: Unknown
en: If he's not the leader, who he is?
zh: 如果他不是领导者，那他是谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: She'd be like, "Glad you asked.
zh: 她会说，“很高兴你问。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mr. Rowntree is an excellent leader because he's very good at instilling in his people the sense of common purpose of what this factory is about.
zh: Rowntree 先生是一位优秀的领导者，因为他非常擅长在他的员工中灌输关于这家工厂是关于什么的共同目标感。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the common purpose rather than Mr. Rowntree himself is their invisible leader."
zh: 而这个共同目标，而不是 Rowntree 先生本人，是他们的隐形领导者。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is maybe the most powerful concept.
zh: 这也许是最有力的概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to manage something as a leader, you have to understand that the most consequential decisions that will affect any organization's life are almost by definition made when no manager is present.
zh: 如果你想作为一个领导者管理某事，你必须明白，影响任何组织生活的最重大的决定，几乎根据定义是在没有经理在场时做出的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You think you made the decision when you told everybody we're going to build a high quality product.
zh: 你以为当你告诉大家我们要打造一个高质量产品时你就做出了决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Our vision is this, our plan is this," but you're not there when the product managers and the designers and the engineers make the actual trade-offs.
zh: “我们的愿景是这个，我们的计划是这个，”但是当产品经理、设计师和工程师做出实际的权衡时你并不在场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Somebody sitting there with the code and being like, "Rounded corners or straight corners, skeuomorphism or not?"
zh: 某人坐在那里面对代码，说，“圆角还是直角，拟物化还是非拟物化？”

speaker: Unknown
en: When person clicks this button, do we double check?
zh: 当用户点击这个按钮时，我们要双重检查吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we understand what they meant or do we just erase their hard drive?
zh: 我们理解他们的意思还是直接擦除他们的硬盘？

speaker: Unknown
en: Thousands upon thousands of these tiny little decisions get made.
zh: 成千上万这样的微小决定被做出。

speaker: Unknown
en: Only the invisible leader is present.
zh: 只有隐形领导者在场。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you don't cultivate that sense of common purpose, you have no control over what's going to happen.
zh: 所以如果你不培养那种共同目标感，你无法控制将要发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, your promises are worthless.
zh: 再说一遍，你的承诺毫无价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you want a little homework, read Mary Parker Follett.
zh: 所以如果你想要一点家庭作业，去读 Mary Parker Follett。

speaker: Unknown
en: She will enlighten you.
zh: 她会启发你。

speaker: Unknown
en: If any of this is at all interesting to you, if you want to explore this, if you want to implement it by Eric's book, Incorruptible, is there a website to look at or is just Google and find it?
zh: 如果这些对你来说有任何有趣之处，如果你想探索这个，如果你想实施它，通过 Eric 的书，Incorruptible，是有网站可以看还是直接谷歌搜索找它？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, of course you can find it anywhere books are sold.
zh: 是的，当然，你可以在任何卖书的地方找到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yes, we do have a website, incorruptible.co.
zh: 是的，我们确实有一个网站，incorruptible.co。

speaker: Unknown
en: Please join the mailing list.
zh: 请加入邮件列表。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have tons of bonus content, especially for those who are implementers.
zh: 我们有大量的额外内容，特别是针对那些实施者。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have implementation guides and advanced implementation guides and readers guides, lots of extra content, including a secret chapter that got cut from the original manuscript.
zh: 我们有实施指南和高级实施指南以及读者指南，很多额外的内容，包括从原稿中删减的一章秘密章节。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tried to make it really worth your while to go to the website pre-order and sign up for the mailing list.
zh: 试着让你去网站预订并注册邮件列表变得非常值得。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you don't have to buy it from me.
zh: 但你不必从我这里买。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could buy the book anywhere books are sold.
zh: 你可以在任何卖书的地方买到这本书。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's in hardcover.
zh: 有精装本。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's in audiobook and eBook.
zh: 有有声书和电子书。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to.
zh: 如果你想的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of my favorite things about the website, we have a list of, more than a hundred last time I counted of local independent bookstores that are carrying the book and where you could order online.
zh: 关于网站我最喜欢的事情之一，我们有一个列表，上次我数的时候超过一百家正在售卖这本书的当地独立书店，你可以在那里在线订购。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you want to, not only could you do me a favor and buy a copy or 10 or 20 or however many you want and give them away, but if you'd like to make the day of your local independent bookstore, you want to support a local community, like a pillar of your community and be their favorite customer, you call them up and say, "I heard this book coming out.
zh: 所以如果你想，不仅可以帮我个忙买一本或 10 本或 20 本或者你想要多少本就买多少本然后送人，而且如果你想让你当地的独立书店开心一天，你想支持当地社区，比如你社区的支柱，并成为他们最喜欢的顾客，你可以给他们打电话说，“我听说这本书要出了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd like a bunch of copies to give away.
zh: 我想要一堆副本送人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I get them on launch day?"
zh: 我能在发售日买到吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Your friends will thank you, the bookstore will thank you and I will thank you.
zh: 你的朋友会感谢你，书店会感谢你，我也会感谢你。

speaker: Unknown
en: A great pitch.
zh: 很棒的推介。

speaker: Unknown
en: Incorruptible: Why Good Companies Go Bad... and How Great Companies Stay Great.
zh: Incorruptible: Why Good Companies Go Bad... and How Great Companies Stay Great.

speaker: Unknown
en: Eric Ries, thank you so much for being here.
zh: Eric Ries，非常感谢你来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hey, thank you, Lenny.
zh: 嘿，谢谢你，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Appreciate it.
zh: 很感激。

speaker: Unknown
en: Appreciate you giving the chance to talk about it and congrats on all you're doing.
zh: 感激你给我机会谈论这个，也祝贺你所做的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks, Eric.
zh: 谢谢，Eric。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bye, everyone.
zh: 再见，大家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢您的收听。

zh: 如果你觉得这很有价值，你可以在 Apple Podcast、Spotify 或你最喜欢的播客应用上订阅这个节目。

en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们评分或留下评论，因为这确实有助于其他听众找到这个播客。

en: You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com.
zh: 你可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到所有过去的集数或了解更多关于节目的信息。

en: See you in the next episode.
zh: 下一集见。
